review:
Когда Миядзаки били в детстве, ремнем замахивались с задержкой чтобы он не увернулся, это все знают. Но мало кто знает продолжение этой великой истории.
Однажды Миядзаки адаптировался к этому, заучил все возможные фреймы ремня, и больше не получал урон. Но его отец не растерялся, и потратил 2 года на то, чтобы снова удивить своего любимого сына. Теперь он наносил в 10 раз больше урона ремнем, прыгал как угорелый по всей комнате, а так же использовал быстрые комбинации ударов (в перемешку со старыми добрыми задержками), от чего глаза Миядзаки не успевали фокусироваться на нем. Но отец знал что одного этого мало, чтобы удивить сына, поэтому теперь его ремень всегда наносил урон по области, на случай если сын захочет перекатиться ему за спину. И даже когда Миядзаки думал что в безопасности сможет попить свою бутылочку фанты, атака ремнем доставала его через всю комнату.
Эта воспоминания так сильно вдохновили Миядзаки, что он посвятил этому целое DLC к своей игре Elden Ring.

review_english: When Miyazaki was beaten as a child, the belt was swung with a delay so that he would not dodge, everyone knows this. But few people know the continuation of this great story. One day Miyazaki adapted to this, learned all possible belt frames, and no longer took damage. But his father was not at a loss, and spent 2 years trying to surprise his beloved son again. Now he was doing 10 times more damage with the belt, jumping like crazy around the room, and also using quick combinations of punches (mixed with good old delays), which made Miyazaki's eyes have no time to focus on him. But the father knew that this alone was not enough to surprise his son, so now his belt always dealt damage in an area, in case his son wanted to roll behind his back. And even when Miyazaki thought he could safely drink his bottle of Fanta, the attack with the belt took him across the room. This memory inspired Miyazaki so much that he dedicated an entire DLC to his game Elden Ring to it.

review:
The ultimate GAMING experience! Honestly, this is one of those cases where writing a review feels unnecessary because words simply can't do it justice.

I hadn’t bought a Collector’s Edition in a long time, but even before this game came out, I knew I had to get the CE of Elden Ring.

The game is perfect, and it belongs in the category of “you absolutely have to play this before you die.” If I really had to mention a negative, it would probably be the reused enemies, which are the game’s biggest flaw. But the gameplay, exploration, and boss fights are all masterclass level! (ER has the weakest PvP among the Souls games, but it's still fun.)

I've been a huge fan of FromSoftware since 2011, and I can confidently say they're the best developers in the industry. 

Perfection.

review:
说实在的，我完全不懂，为什么法环里面这些NPC也依旧是没有善终的。
你要是说黑魂系列，火要灭了，大家都得完蛋，世界马上要完蛋了。这种末世背景下，大家都很惨那很正常。
但是交界地有到这种程度吗？交界地的事说白了就是玛丽卡这一家打内战。可以说说是乱世，但完全不到灭世的地步。这样的世界观下，居然NPC也基本没有好的结局。
柯林疯了，d和他的孪生兄弟死了，菲雅死了，罗杰尔死了，铁匠修古也要死了，罗德莉卡好不容易脱离了接肢的命运，还不容易走上自己作为调灵师的道路，居然要留在圆桌和修谷一起赴死。就连解指婆婆恩雅，最支持我们的人，也随着死亡卢恩的释放死去了。整个大赐福的人几乎死光了，唯一幸终的是涅斐丽，甚至成为了史东薇尔新王。
再说其他NPC，对我们最好的魔法老师瑟濂，明明都已经重返学院，要重兴源流了，却化作魔法师球了。米莉森自杀了，虾哥被食粪者诅咒，拉雅要带着被整个交界地歧视的蛇人的身份，孤独的活下去。布莱德，伊吉，他们都死了。亚历山大明明已经成为强者，却还是输掉了和我们的决斗，破碎了。霍斯劳放弃了成为英雄的愿望，却死在了壶村。
巨人山顶上，梅琳娜牺牲自己点燃了黄金树。
也许三指真的是对的。

review_english: To be honest, I don't understand why these NPCs in the Ring of Magic still don't have a happy ending. If you talk about the Dark Souls series, the fire is going out, everyone has to die, and the world is about to end. In this apocalyptic background, it is normal for everyone to be miserable, but is it to this extent in the junction? To put it bluntly, the things in the junction are Marika's family fighting a civil war. It can be said to be a troubled world, but it is not at all the level of annihilation. In such a world view, there are basically no good endings for NPCs. Colin is crazy. d and his twin brother are dead, Feiya is dead, Roger is dead, and the blacksmith Xiugu is also going to die. Roderica finally escaped the fate of having a limb, and it is not easy for her to embark on her own path as a spirit adjuster. She actually has to stay at the Round Table and die with Xiugu. Even Enya, the mother-in-law who supported us the most, also died with the release of the death rune. Almost all the people in the Great Blessing died, and the only one who died happily was Neferi. He even became the new king of Stonewell. Not to mention that other NPCs were jealous of our best magic teacher. He had obviously returned to the academy to revive the source, but turned into a magician ball. Millicent committed suicide. Brother Shrimp was cursed by the coprophagous man. Raya wanted to live alone as a snake-man who was discriminated against by the entire border. Brad, Iggy, they were all dead. Alexander had become a strong man, but he still lost the duel with us. It was broken. Khosrow gave up his desire to become a hero but died in the Pot Village on the top of Giant Mountain. Melina sacrificed herself to light the golden tree. Maybe Three Fingers was really right.

review:
[h3]A generational game, but not without flaws[/h3]

To preface this review: my only experience with any souls-like was 'The First Berserker: Khazan', and I never finished it.
Actually, I almost never finished Elden Ring either. A few hours in, and I was ready to quit, but the sheer number of players glazing this game was bugging me. "What do they see, that i dont?''
I couldn't tell you exactly when I found my answer; it was more of a gradual experience. But I do now understand, even if I don't fully agree.

The [b][i]world[/i][/b] that's built here is amazing. The ambience and overall vibes made me feel like this was a living, breathing realm full of despair and danger. Even without many cut scenes, I could still grasp the severity and gravity of the events that had taken place. (even if most of the time i had no idea what was happening in the story)

The [b][i]combat[/i][/b], while at times was a little frustrating, is still one of its best features. The build diversity and scope are something special that I can't really find a comparison to in anything else I've played before it. 
The bosses, too, were imaginative and challenging, and more than a few times I found myself jumping out of my seat and slamming my desk in excitement after I defeated them.

There's more I could praise, but I won't, because so many reviews have already done that. So instead ill bring up a few of the [b][i]negatives[/i][/b] that i dont see mentioned very often.
The target locking in this game is dreadful. I swear I died more times to this than actual bosses. It's mostly fine when you have a single enemy to deal with, but as soon as there are multiple targets, it fails miserably.
The other massive flaw I feel this game has is with its in-game systems/mechanics. I can't think of a single game I've ever played that required me to tab out and Google as many times as this. It really was ridiculous. There is just so much that isn't explained or explained well, and most players will miss out on a lot of content or waste a lot of time without Alt-tabbing and turning to guides. 

Overall though, I'm happy I put the time in and finished it. It was a great adventure.

⠀

review:
Bonne introduction aux Souls, qui fut pour moi une première relativement longue et prenante, mais réalisable. 

La recette reste la même : une sacrée famille dysfonctionnelle, un feu à raviver et cette fatidique question : faut-il réparer un monde déjà brisé ? 
Elden Ring reprend les mécaniques et l'esthétique de ses prédécesseurs dans une cartographie inédite, où l'immensité de l'Entre-Terre laisse libre cours à l'exploration. 

300 heures et 300 murs frappés plus tard, je considère que c'était (vraiment) mémorable.

review_english: Good introduction to Souls, which was a relatively long and demanding first time for me, but achievable. Elden Ring takes up the mechanics and aesthetics of its predecessors in a new cartography, where the immensity of Inter-Earth gives free rein to exploration. 300 hours and 300 wall hits later, I consider it (really) memorable.

review:
Positiv.

Ich habe Elden Ring gekauft.

Der erste Gegner hat mich verprügelt.

Der erste Boss hat mich verprügelt.

Normale Gegner haben mich verprügelt.

Die Umgebung hat mich verprügelt.

Meine eigene Dummheit hat mich verprügelt.

Ca.160 Stunden später wurde ich immer noch verprügelt.

10/10.

Elden Ring schafft etwas, das nur wenige Spiele schaffen: Es bestraft dich für Fehler, belohnt dich für Ausdauer und gibt dir das Gefühl, jede einzelne Herausforderung wirklich selbst überwunden zu haben.

Die Spielwelt ist riesig, voller Geheimnisse und belohnt Neugier an jeder Ecke. Jeder Sieg fühlt sich verdient an. Jeder Boss bleibt im Gedächtnis. Jede Entdeckung motiviert dazu, noch einen Hügel, noch eine Höhle und noch ein Schloss zu erkunden.

Ich habe geflucht.

Ich habe gelacht.

Ich habe an meinen Fähigkeiten gezweifelt.

Und ich habe jede Minute davon genossen.

Ein Meisterwerk, das seinesgleichen sucht.

Jetzt entschuldigt mich bitte.

Ich bin gestorben.

review_english: Positive. I bought Elden Ring. The first opponent beat me up. The first boss beat me up. Normal opponents beat me up. The environment beat me up. My own stupidity beat me up. About 160 hours later I was still being beaten. 10 10. Elden Ring does something that few games do. It punishes you for mistakes, rewards you for perseverance, and makes you feel like you've truly overcome every single challenge. The game world is huge, full of secrets and rewards curiosity at every corner. Every victory feels earned. Every boss is memorable. Every discovery motivates you to explore another hill, another cave and another castle. I cursed. I laughed. I doubted my abilities. And I enjoyed every minute of it. A masterpiece that is second to none. Now please excuse me. I died.

Everywhere is in ruins, the ruins are falling apart due to lack of maintenance, the roads are like molehills and there is a sect dungeon at every corner. While the palaces are swimming in luxury, the salaries of the soldiers in the castles have probably not been paid for months, the men are so hungry and nervous that you salute, they draw swords, you pass the road and the dragon is breathing fire. Still, it is immensely satisfying to gradually become stronger in this unworthy system and line up all these aggressive tradesmen and corrupt Presidents. I left my mental health here but it was worth it, 10 10 masterpieces.

review:
[h1]「あっちに何かある」で一生遊べる。[/h1]

やはりフロムゲーは面白い。

最初にリムグレイブへ出た瞬間の、
「あ、これ絶対ヤバいやつだ」
というワクワク感が凄かった。

今作はオープンワールドになったことで、
探索の楽しさがとにかく強い。

遠くに見える建物、
怪しい洞窟、
意味深な廃墟。

「ちょっと寄るか」
で寄り道してるだけで数時間消える。

しかも、
その寄り道にちゃんと意味があるのが凄い。

新装備、
強敵、
イベント、
知らない土地。

探索そのものがご褒美になっている。

戦闘もやっぱり面白い。

敵の動きを見て、
攻撃を覚えて、
少しずつ攻略していく。

最初は無理ゲーに見えたボスを倒せた時の達成感は、
やっぱりフロムゲーならでは。

何回負けても、
「次はいけるかも」
と思わせてくる。

あとビルドの自由度がかなり高い。

脳筋、
魔法、
祈祷、
状態異常、
変態ビルド。

人によって全然プレイスタイルが違うのも面白い。

もちろん、
難易度は高い。

普通にボコボコにされるし、
理不尽に感じる瞬間もある。

でも、
その苦労込みで記憶に残る。

気づけば攻略情報を漁り、
気づけばまたボスに挑み、
気づけば数十時間経っている。

やっぱり、
フロムゲーは面白い。

review:
宫崎英高的生涯集大成之作。

瑰丽诡谲的美术塑造、神乎其神的箱庭设计、精巧绝伦的战斗构筑与数值设计配合以宏大史诗的世界观构筑以及炉火纯青的场景叙事技巧、独特的碎片化叙事共同塑造了艾尔登法环这样一个生命周期长到难以想象的神作。

即使通关四周目许久之后，我一直没有打开过游戏，我也仍然热衷于参与法环社区对于世界观背景的讨论，各家观点层出不穷，总是能为我个人对法环世界观的认识带来新的看法。

我对这个世界的了解越深，我就越能感受到我在这样一个宏大的世界中切切实实地冒险过。

感谢FS社，感谢马丁，感谢环学社区中一直发光的大佬们，为我带来的一场前所未有且此生难忘的盛大冒险。

Even though I have not opened the game for a long time after completing the four rounds, I am still keen to participate in the discussion of the world view background in the Ring community. Ich habe unzählige Male ins Gras gebissen, und manche Bosse haben mich echt an den Rand des Wahnsinns getrieben. Dass man bei absolutem Frust einfach umdrehen, woanders leveln oder Geisteraschen zur Hilfe rufen konnte, hat mir am Ende wohl das Leben gerettet.Trotz der brutalen Härte hat mich die Open World komplett umgehauen. Dieses freie Erkunden ohne nervige Symbole auf der Karte und die gigantischen unterirdischen Städte waren einfach nur heftig. Klar, manche Mini-Dungeons haben sich wiederholt, aber das grandiose Artdesign hat alles rausgerissen. Auch mit Abstand bleibt mein Fazit: Es war unheimlich schwer, aber jeder Wutanfall hat sich für dieses Brett von einem Spiel gelohnt.

review:
Elden Ring took everything that made Dark Souls great, dialled it up a bunch of notches, and set you loose in an open world.

I wasn't initially sold on the idea of an Open world souls game, but I can honestly say that I think it was a fantastic move to make the genre accessible to new players. Far more so than in the souls series, if you hit a wall you can often pick another direction very easily, find new dungeons or encounters, and hunt for loot and runes without specifically banging your head against a wall. Pair this with the spirit ashes allowing summons in almost every boss fight that you can level with you, and this is a great starting point for newcomers to the genre.

If you're a veteran, the game has you covered too, since there's no NEED to engage with summons if you don't want to. The fights escalate a lot as the game goes on, and really make you feel rewarded for learning the dances in a way I feel is only beaten by Sekiro in the Genre.

review:
Se tem um jogo que marcou minha vida como jogador, esse jogo foi Elden Ring. 

Eu posso dizer sem exagero que ele é o meu jogo favorito. Desde o momento em que comecei a explorar as Terras Intermédias, fiquei completamente fascinado pelo mundo, pela liberdade de exploração e pela sensação constante de descoberta. É um daqueles jogos raros que conseguem fazer você perder horas sem nem perceber.

Platinar Elden Ring foi uma das experiências mais gratificantes que já tive, cada conquista parecia realmente merecida. Não foi apenas uma questão de completar objetivos, mas de viver uma jornada gigantesca, cheia de desafios, aprendizado e momentos memoráveis.

Uma das coisas que mais gostei foi derrotar todos os chefes opcionais que encontrei pelo caminho. Muitos deles me fizeram passar raiva, morrer dezenas de vezes e questionar minhas escolhas de build, mas a sensação de finalmente vencer cada um deles era indescritível. Cada vitória parecia uma recompensa por toda a dedicação e persistência.

A exploração foi outro ponto que me conquistou completamente. Fiz questão de explorar cada canto do mapa, entrar em cavernas escondidas, enfrentar inimigos secretos, descobrir áreas subterrâneas impressionantes e encontrar itens que muitos jogadores provavelmente deixaram passar. Sempre havia algo novo para descobrir, e isso tornou a aventura ainda mais especial.

Também adorei a variedade de armas e builds. Testei várias combinações diferentes ao longo da minha jornada, experimentando estilos de jogo completamente distintos. Em um momento eu estava focado em força bruta, em outro usando destreza, magia, fé ou combinações híbridas. Essa liberdade de criar personagens tão diferentes fez com que o jogo continuasse divertido mesmo após centenas de horas.

Elden Ring me proporcionou de tudo: momentos de estresse, derrotas frustrantes, surpresas incríveis e muitas risadas. Teve situações em que eu fiquei preso em um chefe por horas, mas também momentos em que algo totalmente inesperado acontecia e eu simplesmente não conseguia parar de rir. Essas experiências fazem parte do que tornou essa jornada tão especial para mim.

Hoje posso dizer que sou completamente viciado nesse jogo. Mesmo depois de terminar, platinar e explorar praticamente tudo o que ele oferece, ainda sinto vontade de voltar, testar novas builds, enfrentar chefes novamente e revisitar lugares que marcaram minha aventura. Por tudo isso, recomendo Elden Ring para qualquer pessoa que goste de jogos desafiadores, exploração e liberdade. É uma obra que consegue recompensar a curiosidade do jogador como poucos jogos conseguem fazer.

E a minha jornada ainda não acabou. Com toda certeza pretendo comprar a DLC Shadow of the Erdtree, porque depois de tudo o que vivi nesse universo, quero continuar explorando cada novo desafio, descobrir novos segredos e criar ainda mais memórias em um dos melhores jogos que já joguei. Elden Ring não foi apenas um jogo para mim; foi uma experiência que dificilmente será esquecida.

review_english: If there is a game that marked my life as a player, it was Elden Ring. I can say without exaggeration that it is my favorite game. From the moment I began exploring the Middle Lands, I was completely fascinated by the world, the freedom of exploration, and the constant sense of discovery. It's one of those rare games that can make you lose hours without even realizing it. Platinuming Elden Ring was one of the most rewarding experiences I've ever had, each achievement felt truly deserved. It was not just a matter of completing objectives, but of living a gigantic journey, full of challenges, learning and memorable moments. One of the things I liked most was defeating all the optional bosses I encountered along the way. Many of them made me angry, die dozens of times and question my build choices, but the feeling of finally beating each one of them was indescribable. Each victory felt like a reward for all the dedication and persistence. Exploration was another point that completely won me over. I made a point of exploring every corner of the map, entering hidden caves, facing secret enemies, discovering impressive underground areas, and finding items that many players probably missed. There was always something new to discover, and that made the adventure even more special. I also loved the variety of weapons and builds. I tested several different combinations throughout my journey, experimenting with completely different play styles. At one moment I was focused on brute force, at another using dexterity, magic, faith or hybrid combinations. This freedom to create such different characters meant that the game remained fun even after hundreds of hours. Elden Ring gave me everything from moments of stress, frustrating defeats, incredible surprises and lots of laughs. There were situations where I was stuck on a boss for hours, but also times when something totally unexpected happened and I just couldn't stop laughing. These experiences are part of what made this journey so special for me. Today I can say that I am completely addicted to this game. Even after finishing, platinumizing and exploring practically everything it offers, I still feel like going back, testing new builds, facing bosses again and revisiting places that marked my adventure. For all of this, I recommend Elden Ring to anyone who likes challenging games, exploration and freedom. It is a work that manages to reward the player's curiosity in a way that few games can do. And my journey is not over yet. I definitely intend to buy the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC, because after everything I've experienced in this universe, I want to continue exploring each new challenge, discover new secrets and create even more memories in one of the best games I've ever played. Elden Ring wasn't just a game for me, it was an experience that will hardly be forgotten.

review:
Люблю цю гру як ніщо інше.
Люблю коли взимку гріє порвана срака
Люблю коли літом кидає в холодний піт після того як наснився Консорт Радан.
Надзвичайно подобається що без прийняття стоїцизму в цьому світі не вижити. Нарешті ігри, які змушують розвиватися.
Це певно єдина гра у всій моїй бібіліотеці, де мене слизькі текстурки вбили більше разів, аніж будь-який бос. 
Ця гра як токсичний колишній, ти знаєш що вже не варто, але щоразу заходиш і кажеш "щас тобі буде пізда Soldier of Godrick", але знову виходиш з поразкою, хоча гра вже навчила, що головне не здаватися і колись ти зможеш увійти до легенд, які здолали Ріка, soldier of God. 
Я дуже рекомендую цю гру людям, у яких всередині є стержень. За тиждень і його не буде. Не буде друзів, батьків, всі покинуть. Але завжди перед вами буде Malenia, blade of Miquella. Дружина, сестра, свекровь і кум в одному лиці. 
Можливо більше не існуватиме рідного "я люблю тебе", зате ви точно знатимете звідки походить фраза "Heed my words. I am Malenia. Blade of Miquella. And I have never known defeat."

Гра класна, рекомендую >.<

Tentei gostar de Elden Ring, mas a fórmula soulslike não funcionou para mim. O mundo é grande, mas muitas vezes parece vazio, e a exploração raramente me pareceu recompensadora.

Grande parte do tempo eu sentia que andava muito para encontrar itens pouco úteis, materiais de crafting ou recompensas que não mudavam muito a experiência. Além disso, várias dungeons e encontros começaram a parecer repetitivos.

Entendo por que muita gente gosta, mas para mim a dificuldade, a exploração e a progressão não compensaram. Foi uma experiência cansativa e pouco satisfatória.

review_english: I tried to like Elden Ring, but the soulslike formula didn't work for me. The world is big, but it often feels empty, and exploration rarely felt rewarding. Much of the time I felt like I had to walk a lot to find less useful items, crafting materials or rewards that didn't change the experience much. Additionally, several dungeons and encounters began to feel repetitive. I understand why a lot of people like it, but for me the difficulty, exploration and progression weren't worth it. It was a tiring and unsatisfying experience.

review:
After 100+ hours in Elden Ring, I can confidently say this game stole my soul, my sleep schedule, and probably a few years off my lifespan.

The bosses? Pure cinema. Some fights made me feel like a legendary warrior. Others made me question every life decision that brought me here. Either way, 10/10 experience. Getting obliterated has never looked this cool.

And the endings? Surprisingly beautiful, emotional, and worth every second. Especially Ranni’s ending (RANNI MOMMY <3).
I abandoned basic human happiness for a blue space witch and I regret absolutely nothing.

review:
I've played Elden Ring more times than I can count, first on PS4 when it launched, then again on PS5, and came back once more when Shadow of the Erdtree dropped, and now on PC, every single run, it still finds a way to impress, the gameplay is the foundation everything else sits on, and FromSoftware absolutely nailed it.

The level design is masterful, each region has its own identity and mood, and legacy dungeons like Stormveil and Leyndell are some of the most carefully crafted spaces I've ever explored in a game. 
The cutscenes are genuinely stunning, the art, the lighting, the scale and the Soundtracks ofc.

If you want to start a FromSoftware game, Elden Ring is your go-to, It's far more beginner friendly than people make it out to be, yes there are hard parts, but the summon system is there for a reason, struggling with a boss? Call for help, It genuinely makes the experience more accessible without taking anything away from it.

And the DLC, At 40€ Shadow of the Erdtree is a steal, the amount of content, new areas, new weapons, and bosses it brings closer to a full sequel than an expansion, If you loved the base game, this is non-negotiable.

review: Игра супер, мой первый соулсборн и я не разочаровался что решил выбрать как первый соулс именно Элден. Мир красивый, боссы прикольные, но многие повторяются, и иногда кажется что мир искусственно растянут, но впечатление остались хорошие. Acabou que eu platinei Elden Ring.

A diversidade do mapa é abursa, todo canto tem coisa pra explorar, muitas rotas pra tomar, além de que você pode fazer o seu próprio caminho.

Dito isso: Jogo super divertido e valeu cada centavo!

review:
Дракон-ЛИЧ, буквально являющийся мертвецом и xyachащий по первому же живому встречному огнём и молнией? - Повышенное сопротивление к святому урону...

Больная баба, гниющая изнутри, которую можно по кускам разобрать и одну половину сдать на металлолом, а вторую по дешевке впарить на чёрный рынок, которая ещё и подвергает такой же участи посмевших оскорбить её внеземную красоту негодников? - Повышенное сопротивление к святому урону...

Ebanый волк, коим-то образом эволюционировавший достаточно, чтобы постучать тебе по твоей черепной коробке своим выкованным из говна и чьих-то явно несвежих останков подобием палки-копалки, как зажравшийся скуфяра xyem по губам prostiтутки? - Повышенное сопротивление к святому урону...

В общем, энд гейм данной игрухи определенного благосклонен к священнослужителям, решившим потратить несколько дней своей жизни на облегчение страданий местных мракобесов, которым уже явно кроме насильного экзорцизма ничего не поможет...

review:
Je recommande avec quelques réserves.
Mon premier vrai soulslike. J'ai fait deux fois le jeu, une fois en coop avec un build foi, et une fois en solo avec un build dextérité. J'ai déjà envie de relancer une run magie et une run force.
J'avais de l'appréhension mais au final si tu rush pas les boss comme un boeuf, et que tu prends le temps de tout explorer, le jeu est largement accessible, même aux gens pas très doué dans les jeux de combat.
Le jeu n'est pas sans défaut, les stats des objets, talismans, incantations et sorcelleries sont très obscures et si tu veux un minimum t'optimiser il faut passer des heures sur le wiki. Ce qui est assez contraignant.
Ca peut être positif pour certain mais pas pour moi, car c'est à un niveau vraiment absurde, des zones ou des objets sont vraiment inaccessibles sans tricher sur un wiki. C'est assez dommage, car une partie du jeu sera visitée que par les complétionnistes acharnés comme moi.
Globalement je trouve que l'équilibre est assez mauvais, les boss tapent beaucoup trop fort et encaissent que dalle. La plupart des combats durent 30 secondes, soit tu arraches le boss, soit tu te fais arracher. Pour moi j'ai first try 90% des boss sans avoir pu voir ce qu'ils proposaient en pattern. Le jeu sanctionne fortement les builds un peu original et rigolo. Si les boss avaient 2x plus de vie et 2x moins de dégâts, le jeu serait, selon moi, beaucoup plus intéressant.
Aussi le crafting ne sert absolument à rien et c'est dommage. les sorts font tout en mieux et les buffs ne se cumulent pas. 
Sinon j'ai adoré le coté exploration et le coté satisfaisant de fracasser du mob à la pelle, j'ai même apprécié le coté frustrant de perdre ses runes, ça oblige à faire attention lors de l'exploration. Et malgré tout ses défauts je suis accroc au jeu. Les vidéos de lore y ont contribué. Bugs très rare.
Pour ce qui est du DLC je l'ai trouvé très décevant et très en deça du jeu de base. A un prix exagéré. Trop sombre, trop vide et un lore encore moins compréhensible que le jeu de base. 
Aussi y'a vraiment trop de recyclage de mobs, je ne pense pas que ce soit ce qui prenne le plus de budget à faire. C'est vraiment abusé parfois. Notamment dans le DLC.

review_english: I recommend with some reservations. My first real soulslike. I played the game twice, once in co-op with a faith build, and once solo with a dexterity build. I already want to restart a magic run and a force run. I was apprehensive, but in the end if you don't rush through bosses like an ox, and you take the time to explore everything, the game is widely accessible, even to people who aren't very good at fighting games. The game is not without flaws, the stats of objects, talismans, incantations and sorceries are very obscure and if you want to optimize yourself at all, you have to spend hours on the wiki. Which is quite restrictive. This can be positive for some but not for me, because it's at a really absurd level, areas or objects are really inaccessible without cheating on a wiki. It's quite a shame, because part of the game will only be visited by die-hard completionists like me. Overall I find that the balance is quite bad, the bosses hit way too hard and take nothing. Most fights last 30 seconds, you either take out the boss or get ripped out. For me I first tried 90 of the bosses without being able to see what they offered in pattern. The game strongly sanctions builds that are a little original and fun. If the bosses had 2x more health and 2x less damage, the game would, in my opinion, be much more interesting. Also crafting is absolutely useless and that's a shame. the spells do everything better and the buffs do not stack. Otherwise I loved the exploration side and the satisfying side of smashing mobs with shovels, I even appreciated the frustrating side of losing your runes, it forces you to be careful when exploring. And despite all its flaws, I'm addicted to the game. The lore videos contributed to that. Very rare bugs. As for the DLC, I found it very disappointing and far below the base game. At an exaggerated price. Too dark, too empty and lore even less understandable than the base game. Also there's really too much recycling of mobs, I don't think that's what takes the most budget to do. It's really overkill sometimes. Especially in the DLC.

review:
Наиграл 250 часов в базовой версии и сейчас прохожу NG+. Параллельно захожу в колизей и помогаю другим игрокам через призыв. Игра за это время вообще не надоела, что для проектов таких масштабов — редкость.

[b]Главное достоинство игры — это геймплей и вариативность.[/b] 
Количество оружия, магии и брони позволяет собрать практически любой билд под свой стиль. Сложность здесь настраивается сама по себе: если босс кажется непроходимым, игра позволяет призвать прах или других игроков. Если хочется классического челленджа, можно идти в одиночку и учить тайминги атак.

[b]Подача сюжета стандартная для студии — она непрямолинейная и скрыта в деталях.[/b] 
Назвать историю однозначно хорошей сложно, для многих она покажется перегруженной и запутанной. Это качественное тяжелое фэнтези, но атмосфера здесь менее мрачная, чем в серии Dark Souls.

[b]Из объективных недостатков:[/b]
[list]
[*] [b]Проблема баланса уровней.[/b] Из-за свободы исследования открытого мира легко случайно перекачаться, из-за чего ключевые боссы могут показаться слишком простыми.
[*] [b]Специфика DLC.[/b] В дополнении баланс сместился в сторону высокой сложности: у боссов много масштабных АОЕ-атак, а сами арены для боя иногда кажутся слишком тесными и неудобными.
[*] [b]Отвратительный сетевой код и PvP-составляющая:[/b] 
Сетевая часть игры выполнена на пещерном уровне. Из-за ужасного пинга и неткода постоянно происходят фантомные попадания (фантомхиты) — по тебе проходит накопление статусов (кровотечение, обморожение), даже если ты идеально увернулся от удара в перекате. Регистрация урона живет своей жизнью, а дикие задержки ломают всю тактику. Самый сок — это когда тебя внезапно швыряет в анимацию бэкстаба, хотя на твоем экране ты уже давно разорвал дистанцию и находишься совершенно в другом месте. Для соревновательного режима в Колизее такой копеечный сетевой код просто неприемлем.
[*] [b]Оптимизация.[/b] Она тут не лучшая. Если играть с трассировкой лучей на максимуме, бывают заметные просадки кадров в больших локациях — сам заметил это на плато Альтус и по дороге к подъемнику Дектуса.
[/list]

В остальном игра сделана качественно: отличный визуальный стиль, сильная музыка, практически нет багов, а многие неудобные механики из прошлых частей разработчики исправили.

[b]Оценка: 8,5 из 10.[/b]

Игра за это время вообще не надоела, что для проектов таких масштабов редкость. b Главное достоинство игры это геймплей и вариативность. b Количество оружия, магии и брони позволяет собрать практически любой билд под свой стиль. Сложность здесь настраивается сама по себе если босс кажется непроходимым, игра позволяет призвать прах или других игроков. Если хочется классического челленджа, можно идти в одиночку и учить тайминги атак. b Подача сюжета стандартная для студии она непрямолинейная и скрыта в деталях. b Назвать историю однозначно хорошей сложно, для многих она покажется перегруженной и запутанной. Это качественное тяжелое фэнтези, но атмосфера здесь менее мрачная, чем в серии Dark Souls. b Из объективных недостатков b list b Проблема баланса уровней. b Из-за свободы исследования открытого мира легко случайно перекачаться, из-за чего ключевые боссы могут показаться слишком простыми. b Специфика DLC. b В дополнении баланс сместился в сторону высокой сложности у боссов много масштабных АОЕ-атак, а сами арены для боя иногда кажутся слишком тесными и неудобными. b Отвратительный сетевой код и PvP-составляющая b Сетевая часть игры выполнена на пещерном уровне. Из-за ужасного пинга и неткода постоянно происходят фантомные попадания (фантомхиты) по тебе проходит накопление статусов (кровотечение, обморожение), даже если ты идеально увернулся от удара в перекате. Регистрация урона живет своей жизнью, а дикие задержки ломают всю тактику. Самый сок это когда тебя внезапно швыряет в анимацию бэкстаба, хотя на твоем экране ты уже давно разорвал дистанцию и находишься совершенно в другом месте. Для соревновательного режима в Колизее такой копеечный сетевой код просто неприемлем. b Оптимизация. b Она тут не лучшая. Если играть с трассировкой лучей на максимуме, бывают заметные просадки кадров в больших локациях сам заметил это на плато Альтус и по дороге к подъемнику Дектуса. list В остальном игра сделана качественно отличный визуальный стиль, сильная музыка, практически нет багов, а многие неудобные механики из прошлых частей разработчики исправили. b Оценка 8,5 из 10. b
review_english: I've played 250 hours in the basic version and am currently playing through NG. At the same time, I go into the coliseum and help other players through calling. During this time I didn’t get tired of the game at all, which is rare for projects of this scale. b The main advantage of the game is the gameplay and variability. b The amount of weapons, magic and armor allows you to assemble almost any build to suit your style. The difficulty here is adjusted on its own; if the boss seems impassable, the game allows you to summon ashes or other players. If you want a classic challenge, you can go alone and learn attack timings. b The presentation of the plot is standard for the studio; it is not straightforward and hidden in the details. b It is difficult to call the story unambiguously good; for many it will seem overloaded and confusing. This is a quality heavy fantasy, but the atmosphere is less dark than the Dark Souls series. b Among the objective shortcomings b list b The problem of level balance. b Because of the freedom to explore the open world, it's easy to accidentally over-level, which can make key bosses seem too easy. b DLC specifics. b In addition, the balance has shifted towards high difficulty; bosses have a lot of large-scale AOE attacks, and the battle arenas themselves sometimes seem too cramped and uncomfortable. b Disgusting network code and PvP component b The network part of the game is made at a cave level. Due to the terrible ping and netcode, phantom hits (phantom hits) constantly occur and statuses accumulate on you (bleeding, frostbite), even if you perfectly dodged the blow while rolling. Damage registration lives its own life, and wild delays ruin all tactics. The best part is when you are suddenly thrown into a backstub animation, although on your screen you have long since broken the distance and are in a completely different place. For the competitive mode in the Colosseum, such cheap netcode is simply unacceptable. b Optimization. b She's not the best here. If you play with ray tracing at maximum, there are noticeable frame drops in large locations; I myself noticed this on the Altus plateau and on the way to the Dectus lift. list Otherwise, the game is made with high quality, excellent visual style, strong music, practically no bugs, and the developers have corrected many inconvenient mechanics from previous parts. b Rating 8.5 out of 10. b

review:
[h1]「エルデの王に俺はなる！」行き先が限定されたオープンフィールド改め、今作は完全なオープンワールド！老舗によるソウルライクの決定版[/h1]

女王マリカの黄金律と豊穣の象徴である黄金樹に依って統治され、狭間の地は大いに繁栄していたが、恵みの根源たるエルデンリングが突如砕けた。
マリカの後継たるデミゴッドたちは、エルデンリングの破片たる大ルーンを手にし、力に溺れて「破砕戦争」を起こし、結果「大いなる意思」に見捨てられてしまう。
英雄ゴッドフレイの末裔たる褪せ人たる主人公は、「大いなる意思」に導かれてエルデンリングを手中に収め、エルデの王になる為に狭間の地に降り立った…。
今作は色濃くソウルシリーズの流れを汲んでおり、ほぼ全ての操作はシリーズそのままだが、広大なフィールド内を駆ける乗馬がいたり、篝火に相当する祝福でファストトラベルや回復、レベルアップが可能。
敵の攻撃で倒れた時は血痕を掬い上げてリカバリできたように、今作でもルーンが残るので拾うことができれば、デメリットなしでリカバリできる。
完全なオープンワールドに変わって、マップを開くことで確認できる道しるべは一応あるが、序盤からエリアロックがなく何処にでも行けてしまうので、過去作より自由度も上がったが難易度も上がってしまった。
攻略エリア以外でも洞窟や地下墓地のような所謂レガシーダンジョンでの探索で、強化素材と携帯型白ファントムとも言える遺灰や戦技が手に入る。

プレイ時間は本編だけで80時間前後、実績は本編のみで42あり、ゲーム進行、NPCイベント、アイテムコレクションなど、アイテムに関してはソウルシリーズと違って全アイテムを揃える必要はなくなり、実績完遂のハードルは低くなった。
ソウルシリーズでの戦闘で明らかに違う事は、ラスボスを含む全ての敵に体勢値が設定されており、チュートリアルではあまり強調されていないが、タメR2、ジャンプR2、戦技などを駆使して体勢を崩させる事で戦況を優位に運べる。
戦技や指輪に変わるアクセサリーの存在も重要で、これらを付け替えるだけでレベルを上げずとも戦局が激変してしまう事もある、かなり自由度の高い作品。
序盤のストームヴィル城でなかなか進めないようなら、しばらくは他で探索してレベル上げや聖杯瓶の使用回数を増やすアイテム収集などすることをお勧めする。

review_english: h1 I will become the king of Elde. The open field has been changed to a limited destination. This work is completely open world. The definitive version of Soul-like by a long-established company. h1 The land between the gaps was very prosperous, ruled by Queen Marika's golden rule and the golden tree, which is a symbol of fertility. However, the Elden Ring, the source of blessings, suddenly shattered. Marika's successors, the demigods, obtained the Great Rune, a fragment of the Elden Ring. Drowning in power, he starts a Shattered War, and as a result is abandoned by the Great Will. The protagonist, a faded descendant of the hero Godfrey, is led by the Great Will to take possession of the Elden Ring and descend to the Land of Intervals in order to become the King of Elde.This game is very much in the vein of the Soul series, and almost all the controls are the same as the series, but there are horses riding around in the vast field. Blessings equivalent to bonfires allow you to fast travel, recover, and level up. Just like when you were defeated by an enemy's attack, you could recover by scooping up the blood stains. In this game too, runes remain, so if you can pick them up, you can recover without any disadvantages. It's now a completely open world, and there are signposts that you can check by opening the map, but there are no area locks from the beginning and you can go anywhere, so it has more freedom than previous games, but it's also more difficult. Outside of the strategy areas, you can get reinforcement materials, ashes that can be called portable white phantoms, and combat skills by exploring so-called legacy dungeons such as caves and catacombs.The play time is around 80 hours in the main story alone.There are 42 achievements in the main story alone.Game progress NPC events, item collection, etc.Unlike the Soul series, it is no longer necessary to have all the items, and the hurdles for completing the achievements have become lower.What is clearly different in the battles in the Soul series All enemies, including the final boss, have a posture value, and although it is not emphasized much in the tutorial, you can use Tame R2, Jump R2, etc. to break their posture and gain an advantage in the battle.Accessories that can be used as combat techniques and rings are also important, and just by changing these, the battle situation can change dramatically without raising the level.It is a work with a high degree of freedom.If you find it difficult to advance in Stormville Castle in the early stages, We recommend that you explore elsewhere for a while and collect items that will increase your level and increase the number of times you can use the Holy Grail Bottle.

review:
Ni siquiera es la experiencia Souls definitiva. Es simplemente uno de los mejores juegos jamás creados. Hay contenido a patadas. Puedes completarlo como tú quieras. Y tiene uno de los mejores mundos abiertos que he jugado nunca (abarrotado de bosses, misiones secundarias, lugares ocultos, etc).

El único pero es que es un mundo abierto que da demasiada libertad para mi gusto. Hasta el punto en el que es muy fácil perderse y no saber el rumbo de la historia principal. Yo recomendaría como primera experiencia pasarlo con alguien que pueda guiarte un mínimo.

Pero en conclusión un 20/10

review_english: It's not even the definitive Souls experience. It is simply one of the best games ever created. There is plenty of content. You can complete it however you want. And it has one of the best open worlds I've ever played (full of bosses, side missions, hidden places, etc). The only but is that it is an open world that gives too much freedom for my taste. To the point where it is very easy to get lost and not know the direction of the main story. I would recommend as your first experience spending it with someone who can guide you a little. But in conclusion a 20 10

review_english: Overall, the game is a bit crazy and a bit cool. I would describe the situations of this title even in simple language. Everyone has probably heard about the chemical compound NaCl and certainly everyone has used it in some way (no details are required), but let's get to the point. NaCl is nothing more than table salt that saved us, humanity, from, for example, preventing the degradation of meat, preventing the crappy taste of average lunches on school trips, etc. Unfortunately, there are also cases where this compound is the cause of domestic violence, which the creator of the game certainly witnessed in his childhood. I'm talking about situations when the creator's mother didn't add enough salt to the soup, so his father had to punish her by beating her (btw, this situation was the inspiration for the segment where, after defeating Orstein in DS1, smough beats the crap out of him). But that's not the point, because you see, NaCl is created through the ionic bond of the two elements that create this compound, i.e. sodium and chlorine. In this particular bond, the chlorine atom takes the valence electron of the sodium atom to create the so-called electron octet. And the analogy is that sodium is this game AND CHLORINE IS THAT FUCKING FIRE GIANT WHO SHE FUCKED UP EVERYTHING THAT WAS COOL IN THIS FUCKING GAME AND INSTEAD OF CREATING A BOND, THIS GARBAGE FUCKING SON OF A FIRE WHORE BROKE ALL THIS SODIUM UP. review:
После хороших соулсов елден ощущается максимально посредственно с максимально сомнительным геймдизайном и решениями. Что уж говорить про откровенно пустой открытый мир с банальным повтором одних и тех же переодетых мобов, боссов, данжей, аванпостов и прочего, во вторую половину игры это все настолько приедается что хочется это все поскорее скипнуть, забрав сомнительную награду в виде камушка на прокачку пушки. боевая система ничем новым не порадовала, по сути у тебя опять легкая атака/сильная атака, а игра опять сводится к тупому затыкиванию босса, попутно спамя перекатом до нужного момента. 
Что касательно лора  елден шагнул назад, опять нн без предыстории который тупо хочет сесть на трон, опять огонь, опять драконы, опять деревья, опять, опять избранная нежить и по структуре ничего не меняется совсем, после дса ощущение одного и того же не покидает тебя.
Абсолютный провал который все схавали

review_english: After good souls, Elden feels as mediocre as possible with the most questionable game design and decisions. What can we say about a frankly empty open world with a banal repetition of the same disguised mobs, bosses, dungeons, outposts and other things, in the second half of the game it all becomes so boring that you want to skip it all as quickly as possible, taking a dubious reward in the form of a pebble for upgrading your gun. The combat system is nothing new; in fact, you again have a light attack, a strong attack, and the game again comes down to stupidly shutting up the boss, simultaneously spamming with a roll until the right moment. As for the lore, Elden stepped back, again with no backstory who stupidly wants to sit on the throne, again fire, again dragons, again trees, again, again the chosen undead and in structure nothing changes at all, after all the feeling of the same thing does not leave you. An absolute failure that everyone grabbed

review:
The best way I can describe this game is it is a bad GREAT game. There is no doubt that the game is great, however as far as great games go, I think this one is bad at it.

Starting with the negatives, the main one for me is just boss repeats. I don't want to fight 3 Margit's or 5 Demi-Human's or whatever. So there are somewhere around 200 bosses I think? But maybe only about 50 of them are unique? And I understand that you can't really have a game with over 200 unique bosses, but I just find it quite lazy and honestly a bit of a turn off. Why are there 2 Godrick's or 3 Morgott fights or whatever, to me it just doesn't make sense and where yeah, you get more overall boss fight time (which is the fun of the souls games) repeating it in this way? No fun.

Another issue I have is the game feels more like a dodge simulator, some bosses feel like they give you no ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ room to attack. It takes me back to DS3 Pontiff where you just never get a damn turn and honestly, I hate that. I'm not saying I want 5 second long openings to get free staggers, but I don't want to have to do 8 dodges, 1 attack, another 6 dodges, 1 attack, but I also don't want to have to trade damage just because I want to attack. I think some bosses aren't balanced well at all for this, and I think the DLC makes it more of a prominent issue.

That said, that's the main negatives for me, a couple small gripes could also be things like I don't find the Catacombs fun, just felt lengthy imo, I didn't really like the whole underground in Siofra, the area was just a bit tedious, and I do wish you could navigate the map a bit faster especially in NG+, going for graces again takes so long. Add a Surge Sprint into this just like Nightreign, would honestly help so much.

All that said, I still think it's a great game. There really is an endless amount of things to do. And one thing I really love is the weapon variety. In Dark Souls, it felt like you could just get a Uchigatana and be set, but in this I actually wanted to try new weapons. Godskin Peeler with Black Flame Tornado was so much fun for me, then things like the Fallingstar Beast Jaw also has such a cool and unique skill to it, I do love the variety in that and love how almost all weapons feel useable. And then there's magic to swap things up even more, even though I think melee is way more fun, the spells were cool and you could get creative. 

Back to bosses, for what fights are actually unique, I think they are great. Some bosses are just obnoxious, like Red Wolf just running away and spamming Glintblades, it does get infuriating but every game is gonna have annoying bosses like that. I think pretty much all Great Rune bosses are cool, I love the final boss, love Radagon, love Godfrey, they are some very enjoyable bosses. 

Overall great game, definitely has potential to be a great great game, but for me the negatives of it really do take it down quite a bit, but that said the game itself is so good that I would still recommend it 100%. 
8/10 game

review:
Anel Pristino é perfeito, a sensação de recompensa por exploração é satisfatória, o leque de bosses é peak do peak, OST FODA. A variação de builds/armas/magias é bem diversa e a lore rica, mas ainda implícita (bem mais intuitiva que a dos outros Souls).

Se fosse pra apontar defeitos, eu diria que o jogo possa se tornar meio cansativo em certas explorações de dungeons, POR TER MUITAS IGUAIS!!! E o repeteco que tem de chefe... até os principais repetem. Mas, pra mim, isso não é suficiente pra abaixar minha nota pra essa bomba aí do Miyazaki.

MINHA NOTA É 10 PORRA! E A RANNI SEMPRE SERÁ A MELHOR PERSONAGEM DESSE JOGO.

MEU TOP 3 DA FROM É:

1 - LARP SOULS II
2 - SEKIRO
3 - ELDEN RING

review_english: Pristine Ring is perfect, the feeling of reward for exploration is satisfying, the range of bosses is peak do peak, FUCKING OST. The variation of magic weapons builds is very diverse and the lore is rich, but still implicit (much more intuitive than that of other Souls). If I were to point out flaws, I would say that the game can become a bit tiring during certain dungeon explorations, BECAUSE THEY HAVE SO MANY THE SAME!!! And the repetition that a boss has... even the main ones repeat it. But, for me, that's not enough to lower my rating for this bomb from Miyazaki. MY RATING IS A FUCKING 10! AND RANNI WILL ALWAYS BE THE BEST CHARACTER IN THIS GAME. MY TOP 3 FROM FROM IS 1 - LARP SOULS II 2 - SEKIRO 3 - ELDEN RING

review:
In my humblest of opinions, Elden Ring is kinda... My favorite is dark souls II so make whatever assumptions you'd like, but after I first beat the game I was left feeling really meh. I couldn't bring myself to replay it the same way I want to replay DS2 or Sekiro, but I have regardless to make new characters or play with friends, just never beaten it again. Fromsoft really pushed themselves with this game, and it shows. They struggled with enemy variety in that I want to say 40% of enemies are reskinned version of other enemies, and infamously, each miniboss appears five or so times at least across the entire game. Exploration doesn't really reward the player with much, with most rewards being basic loot you can find on enemies or flowers or berry bushes and stuff. The world, however, feels a little empty. Even though dying worlds are their formula, it's really out of place here in my opinion. The game's lore revolves around the fact that the lands between are a lived in world full of colorful diverse characters, and yet I found most NPCs woefully out of place. The capitol was a huge let down for me, for example, and the roundtable hold is plain strange in my opinion, with merchants who you'll want to move between (one selling smithing stones and the other providing upgrades) being on polar opposite sides of the hub... Oh my lord and the dungeons - the dungeons as a whole are a perfect example for how tired I think the devs were while working on this game because the dungeons are the lamest and least inspired parts of this game. They are essentially the same structure with the same enemies for the most part, with ♥♥♥♥ loot at the end oh my lord. Each of them follows the same formula as well, really sad in my opinion. 

Fromsoft tried to accomplish A LOT of things with this title and I think they landed securely in an in-between place where there are great and egregious things at once, but mostly, the game is good.

I want to throw some serious shade at Shadow of the Erdtree DLC. What the hell man. It was just a slog, the areas were uninteresting, others felt uninspired and kind of pointless. The boss movesets seemed AI generated if I'm being honest, felt so unpredictable. Maybe I just wasn't good enough but holy hell the amount of shockwaves and double hit attacks (where you dodge the first part but get hit by the second while you're coming out of a roll) were commonplace. The hippo boss was insane, and I mean that in a good and bad way. It's because of all these facets I never actually reached Mesmer, just got so bogged down by those aforementioned aspects compounding onto me throughout my playtime. I've beaten Nioh 1 and 2 and those have some INSANE and irritating bosses but Shadow of the Erdtree gave me little to feel excited about in terms of pushing through. Oh my lord and the DAMAGE they deal is bonkers. Unironically, my favorite boss in all of Elden Ring was Godrick the Grafted - so much personality, BEST soundtrack in the game IMO, cool concept and phases, but whose moveset and behavior didn't feel chaotic, or plain silly like some of the bosses felt. Call me a normie, but that's my hill I guess.

Anyways after all that ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, it's still a fantastic game, just a flawed one, as they all are. I hope that Fromsoft either improves on their tried and true formula with ideas to keep it fresh (meaning dead worlds etc) or either tries really hard to remedy what made Elden Ring suffer in my eyes - feeling empty and feeling un-lived in. I also hope they take a look back at their masterpieces like Sekiro to reach a better synthesis of their different combat systems. Elden Ring at times could've really benefitted from being more like Sekiro, but instead just felt like DS3, even with guard counters.

review_english: In my humblest of opinions, Elden Ring is kinda... My favorite is dark souls II so make whatever assumptions you'd like, but after I first beat the game I was left feeling really meh. I couldn't bring myself to replay it the same way I want to replay DS2 or Sekiro, but I have regardless to make new characters or play with friends, just never beaten it again. Fromsoft really pushed themselves with this game, and it shows. They struggled with enemy variety in that I want to say 40 of enemies are reskinned version of other enemies, and infamously, each miniboss appears five or so times at least across the entire game. Exploration doesn't really reward the player with much, with most rewards being basic loot you can find on enemies or flowers or berry bushes and stuff. The world, however, feels a little empty. Even though dying worlds are their formula, it's really out of place here in my opinion. The game's lore revolves around the fact that the lands between are a lived in world full of colorful diverse characters, and yet I found most NPCs woefully out of place. The capitol was a huge let down for me, for example, and the roundtable hold is plain strange in my opinion, with merchants who you'll want to move between (one selling smithing stones and the other providing upgrades) being on polar opposite sides of the hub... Oh my lord and the dungeons - the dungeons as a whole are a perfect example for how tired I think the devs were while working on this game because the dungeons are the lamest and least inspired parts of this game. They are essentially the same structure with the same enemies for the most part, with loot at the end oh my lord. Each of them follows the same formula as well, really sad in my opinion. Fromsoft tried to accomplish A LOT of things with this title and I think they landed securely in an in-between place where there are great and egregious things at once, but mostly, the game is good. I want to throw some serious shade at Shadow of the Erdtree DLC. What the hell man. It was just a slog, the areas were uninteresting, others felt uninspired and kind of pointless. The boss movesets seemed AI generated if I'm being honest, felt so unpredictable. Maybe I just wasn't good enough but holy hell the amount of shockwaves and double hit attacks (where you dodge the first part but get hit by the second while you're coming out of a roll) were commonplace. The hippo boss was insane, and I mean that in a good and bad way. It's because of all these facets I never actually reached Mesmer, just got so bogged down by those aforementioned aspects compounding onto me throughout my playtime. I've beaten Nioh 1 and 2 and those have some INSANE and irritating bosses but Shadow of the Erdtree gave me little to feel excited about in terms of pushing through. Oh my lord and the DAMAGE they deal is bonkers. Unironically, my favorite boss in all of Elden Ring was Godrick the Grafted - so much personality, BEST soundtrack in the game IMO, cool concept and phases, but whose moveset and behavior didn't feel chaotic, or plain silly like some of the bosses felt. Call me a normie, but that's my hill I guess. Anyways after all that , it's still a fantastic game, just a flawed one, as they all are. I hope that Fromsoft either improves on their tried and true formula with ideas to keep it fresh (meaning dead worlds etc) or either tries really hard to remedy what made Elden Ring suffer in my eyes - feeling empty and feeling un-lived in. I also hope they take a look back at their masterpieces like Sekiro to reach a better synthesis of their different combat systems. Elden Ring at times could've really benefitted from being more like Sekiro, but instead just felt like DS3, even with guard counters.

review:
"Cuidado com velho maluco"

O pináculo da série Souls. Jogo incrível!

Mundo aberto gigantesco com muita coisa para achar e ampla variedade de inimigos e estilos de jogo, mesmo com um guia em mãos é fácil deixar alguma coisa pra trás. A dificuldade no geral é mais leniente que os outros jogos da série Souls, mas há uma ordem esperada de progressão ao longo das áreas do jogo, que se não for respeitada vai tornar a experiência mais traumática. Os gráficos não são incríveis, mas a ambientação é muito boa. A história como sempre é críptica e os fatos são coletados peça por peça em tudo que é lugar, e os finais são bem enxutos.

O jogo rendeu em torno de 150 horas na primeira partida, tentei explorar e coletar tudo ao máximo mas ainda deixei coisas pra trás. Você pode jogar novamente no NG+ para explorar outras builds e coletar o que não pegou antes, então a rejogabilidade é garantida.

Nota final: Seria fatiado como um peixe pela Malenia novamente / 10

Huge open world with lots to find and a wide variety of enemies and play styles, even with a guide in hand it's easy to leave something behind. The difficulty in general is more lenient than other games in the Souls series, but there is an expected order of progression throughout the game's areas, which if not respected will make the experience more traumatic. review:
Entiendo que el contenido de la [b][i]Tarnished Edition[/i][/b] es parte del lanzamiento del juego en switch 2, por lo que deberíamos agradecer que no sea exclusivo de ésta y llegue a consolas y PC, pero que tengamos que pagar por ello me parece surrealista y una mala señal sobre el futuro de FromSoftware. A esto le añadimos que el juego completo costará en esa consola 20€ menos que en Steam.

Si sólo fuera contenido cosmético para Torrentera pues vale, pero también introduce nuevas clases de inicio, armadura y armas.
Sí, serán sólo unos 3-4€, pero el problema no es el precio sino el mensaje que manda y el precedente que marca. Por muy barato que sea, no recompenséis con vuestro dinero lo que son simple y llanamente decisiones turbias que conforman prácticas anti-consumidor.

[h2]Solución:[/h2]

La gente que no pilló el dlc [b][i]Shadow of the Erdtree[/i][/b] podía conseguir objetos de éste si quien sí lo hizo los dropeaba en multijugador.
Al no checkear si posees el dlc para tener dichos objetos en el inventario, ésto permite obtenerlos gratuitamente con Cheat Engine y una tabla actualizada. Tan sólo esperad y [i]voilà[/i].

review:
Elden Ring est mon tout premier Souls-like, et après avoir passé des dizaines d'heures dessus et approché de ma première fin, je comprends enfin pourquoi les jeux de FromSoftware sont autant appréciés.

La première chose qui m'a marqué est l'ambiance. Les musiques sont excellentes et accompagnent parfaitement l'exploration ainsi que les combats de boss. Visuellement, le jeu est magnifique, avec des paysages impressionnants qui donnent constamment envie de découvrir ce qui se cache derrière la prochaine colline ou dans le prochain donjon.

Côté gameplay, le jeu est exigeant mais extrêmement satisfaisant. Certains boss possèdent des patterns complexes qui demandent de l'observation, de la patience et de la persévérance. Chaque victoire procure un véritable sentiment d'accomplissement, ce qui rend l'aventure particulièrement addictive.

J'ai également été agréablement surpris par l'optimisation. Malgré l'immensité du monde ouvert, l'expérience est restée fluide et agréable pendant toute ma progression.

Le contenu est colossal : exploration, secrets, quêtes, boss optionnels, équipements, builds... Il y a toujours quelque chose à faire ou à découvrir. Même après avoir presque terminé le jeu, j'ai encore l'impression d'avoir raté une multitude de choses.

À tel point que j'envisage carrément de le platiner. Pour un premier Souls, c'est une expérience exceptionnelle qui m'a complètement convaincu. Elden Ring vaut largement son prix et m'a donné envie d'aller encore plus loin dans l'univers de FromSoftware en découvrant les jeux de la série Dark Souls ainsi que Sekiro.

Une aventure mémorable que je recommande sans hésiter. 👍

review_english: Elden Ring is my very first Souls-like, and after spending dozens of hours on it and approaching my first ending, I finally understand why FromSoftware's games are so popular. The first thing that struck me was the atmosphere. The music is excellent and perfectly accompanies the exploration as well as the boss fights. Visually, the game is stunning, with impressive landscapes that constantly leave you wanting to discover what lies behind the next hill or in the next dungeon. Gameplay-wise, the game is demanding but extremely satisfying. Some bosses have complex patterns that require observation, patience and perseverance. Each victory provides a real feeling of accomplishment, which makes the adventure particularly addictive. I was also pleasantly surprised by the optimization. Despite the vastness of the open world, the experience remained fluid and enjoyable throughout my progression. The content is colossal exploration, secrets, quests, optional bosses, equipment, builds... There is always something to do or discover. Even after almost finishing the game, I still feel like I missed out on a multitude of things. So much so that I'm actually considering platinuming it. For a first Souls, it's an exceptional experience that completely convinced me. Elden Ring is well worth its price and made me want to go even further into the FromSoftware universe by discovering the games in the Dark Souls series as well as Sekiro. A memorable adventure that I recommend without hesitation.

review:
Challenging, beautiful, vast, sad, funny, unfair, fascinating. 
These are all terms that came to mind in my playthrough of Elden Ring. At the time of writing I have not yet completed the DLC but I can wholeheartedly say that this is my favorite game of all time.

The game wants to be discovered and rediscovered, and with good reason. You never really feel like you're done in the Lands Between. Keep looking and you'll find a cave, a chest, an NPC or an item you didn't find last time you looked there.
I highly recommend going into this game with as little knowledge of the story and content as possible.

Truly phenomenal.

review_english: I walked through it step by step for 400 hours in 230 PS on PC, including DLC. The environment, the sense of discovery and the views are great but the management is a disaster. Everywhere is in ruins, the ruins are falling apart due to lack of maintenance, the roads are like molehills and there is a sect dungeon at every corner. While the palaces are swimming in luxury, the salaries of the soldiers in the castles have probably not been paid for months, the men are so hungry and nervous that you salute, they draw swords, you pass the road and the dragon is breathing fire. Still, it is immensely satisfying to gradually become stronger in this unworthy system and line up all these aggressive tradesmen and corrupt Presidents. I left my mental health here but it was worth it, 10 10 masterpieces. It's as beautiful as the conversation you had until 5 in the morning on the first day you met...

review:
Legendary game. A masterpiece of a singleplayer game, with a bit of co-op and pvp thrown in there too.

The only real critique I personally have, is that the game is not playable at above 60 fps. I feel like today that is an actual issue, as most people with good PCs are playing games at 120+ fps and it is a big difference in smoothness and visual quality imo. 

Nevertheless, I used Lossless Scaling app to activate framegen and can play it at 120 fps that way, it works perfectly. After you have played the game once, I also highly recommend a remake mod called Elden Ring Reforged. It makes the experience better and is very easy to install. 

This game is a 10/10.

review:
Before Elden Ring, I had never played a soulslike game. Now, after 59 hours in my first real playthrough, I finished the game. First thing I’ll say: the hype was deserved. The game is well made and my journey through [i]The Lands Between[/i]  a TON of fun. The difficulty did not hold me back at all, thanks to the generous help the game gives to the player. Are you a gamer and do you think Elden Ring will be too difficult for you? Don’t worry, just get the game. It’ll be fine.

Bullet points first, then my full spoiler-free review.

- Graphics: 8.5/10
- Exploration: 10/10
- Combat: 9/10
- Bosses: 9/10
- Audio: 9/10
- Story: 8/10
- Fun: ~9/10
- Difficulty: tough but doable

I know everyone always hypes up Elden Ring’s bosses and combat, and while they were incredibly good indeed, my personal highlight of the game was its exploration. There are so many unique areas in the game, and exploring them never feels dull: there’s LOOT everywhere, there’re QUESTS everywhere, there are DUNGEONS everywhere. The whole world is just so meticulously filled with details and things to do, that I just never got bored. My personal favorite area HAS to be [spoiler] Liurnia (duh)… Unfathomably fun. I was obsessed. [/spoiler]

My favorite part of the game is honestly just that the gameplay loop is so fun. It’s just a constant change between exploring, fighting and questing; it never gets boring. The messages people leave behind are also very fun: many of them are very funny or incredibly useful. I’d have missed so many secrets if it weren’t for messages leading me to them… It’s a wonderful replacement of using google to find your way around in the game.

The story and worldbuilding just add onto the pure EPICNESS of Elden Ring. I truly felt like a dark fantasy hero in a broken world. And the way everything matches up, everything works together towards the narrative; it’s just fantastic. 

The world itself is beautiful, we know that already, but also just incredibly BOMBASTIC and OVERWHELMING. It’s honestly a good example of the feeling of the Sublime; certain areas and places are just beyond magnificent, are just awe-inspiring on so many levels, and just so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ gigantic. Oftentimes I wanted to go take a screenshot of some landscape but everything is just so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ big so the screenshots never truly do the game justice. 

And now: the difficulty. The bosses are definitely hard, but thanks to the many tools the game gives you, I could beat almost every boss with less than 15 tries, often averaging 3-5 tries. And that’s not because I’m an experienced soulslike or competitive player; it’s thanks to the buffs and the loadouts you can get & thanks to the summons of the game. Otherwise I would’ve probably quit the game way before finishing it. Long live [spoiler] Moonveil & Skeleton Soldiers :)! [/spoiler]

Last few notes;
-	59 hours
-	32 achievements
-	Level 150
-	16 march – 16 may
-	Half on Steam Deck/half on PC
-	[spoiler]Ranni, Millicent, Kalé, Hewg, Roderika, Rogier, Blaidd [/spoiler] = MY GOATS
-	Fav bosses: [spoiler]Rykard, Elden Beast, Mohg, Godrick, Agheel, Godfrey[/spoiler]
-	Hated bosses: [spoiler]Deathbird, Baleful Shadow, Melania[/spoiler]
-	Hardest shardbearers: [spoiler]Melania (46), Mohg (20), Elden Beast (15)[/spoiler]
-	Easiest shardbearers: [spoiler]Renalla (1), Margit (2), Godrick (3)[/spoiler]

review: 宫崎英高小时候刚出生喊了一声爸爸，结果他母亲的支线断掉了，从此宫崎英高再也没有见过他的妈妈。宫崎英高小时候写作业写着写着睡着了，被尿憋醒后发现作业刷新了，顾不了那么多，转身去了厕所，结果发现厕所门不能从这一侧打开，没有办法他只能翻窗户，结果翻窗户的时候被旁边的树枝扫到了地上的洞里。宫崎英高从洞里出来发现要翻过三座山，趟过两条河，穿过一片丛林再走进一个乌漆嘛黑的隧道里跑上8公里才能用望远镜看到自己家的屋顶。宫崎英高想要就地解决但是发现必须触摸马桶盖儿才能上厕所。宫崎英高最后终于翻进了厕所，结果厕所天花板上躲着5只石像小怪，历经千辛万苦，他终于可以上厕所了，结果发现马桶会喷火。宫崎英高动静太大引来他爸爸的不满，他的爸爸拿出七匹狼快慢抽，宫崎英高只能来回翻滚，但是他爸会读指令，等宫崎英高翻滚完的那一瞬间，上去就是一皮带三连击。宫崎英高想要逃回自己的房间，结果发现自己必须要在八个弓箭手的围攻下点燃八盏灯才能返回房的门口，宫崎英高终于回到了房门口，结果发现门不能从这一侧打开，他只能又翻起窗户，然后又被同一个树枝甩进了同一个洞里

review_english: When Hidetaka Miyazaki was a child, he called him daddy when he was just born. As a result, his mother's branch line was cut off. Miyazaki Hidetaka never saw his mother again. When Hidetaka Miyazaki was a child, he fell asleep while writing homework. After he woke up from peeing, he found that the homework had been refreshed. He couldn't care so much, so he turned around and went to the toilet. He found that the toilet door could not be opened from this side. He had no choice but to climb through the window. When he climbed through the window, he was swept by a branch next to him into a hole in the ground. Miyazaki Hidetaka came out of the hole and found that he had to climb over three mountains. He waded through two rivers, walked through a jungle, and then walked into a pitch-black tunnel and ran for 8 kilometers before he could see the roof of his home with a telescope. Miyazaki wanted to solve it on the spot, but found that he had to touch the toilet seat to go to the toilet. Miyazaki finally climbed into the toilet. It turned out that there were five stone monsters hiding on the toilet ceiling. After all the hardships, he was finally able to go to the toilet. It turned out that the toilet would spit fire. Miyazaki made too much noise, which made his father dissatisfied. His father took out the septwolves and pumped them quickly. Hidetaka Miyazaki could only roll back and forth, but his father could read the instructions. The moment Miyazaki Hidetaka finished rolling, he went up and struck three times with a belt. Hidetaka Miyazaki wanted to escape back to his room, but found that he had to light eight lamps under the siege of eight archers before he could return to the door of the room. Hidetaka Miyazaki finally returned to the door of the room, but found that the door could not be opened from this side. He could only flip up the window again, and was thrown into the same hole by the same branch.

review:
Elden Ring》真的属于那种“又折磨人又停不下来”的游戏。刚开始玩的时候，你可能会被Boss两刀砍死，然后开始怀疑人生，但等你终于打赢的时候，那个爽感是真的上头。人类可能天生就喜欢受苦，不然这游戏也不会这么火。

游戏的开放世界特别大，而且不是那种无聊的大。你随便乱逛都有可能发现隐藏地牢、奇怪NPC，或者突然冒出来一个比房子还大的怪物狠狠干你一顿。整个探索过程特别有惊喜感。

战斗系统还是 FromSoftware 那种经典高难度风格，操作需要反应和耐心，但每次成功闪避、反杀Boss的时候都特别有成就感。你还能玩不同流派，拿大剑莽、玩魔法远程轰炸，或者当个阴间刺客，都挺有意思。

画面和音乐也很顶，尤其是Boss战的时候，音乐一响直接压迫感拉满。有些场景甚至帅到你会停下来截图，虽然下一秒可能就被怪偷袭打死。开发者对玩家显然没什么怜悯心。

不过这游戏也不是人人都能接受。如果你不喜欢高难度，或者没耐心一直死了重来，那玩起来可能会很坐牢。但如果你能接受挑战，那《Elden Ring》绝对是一款能让人沉迷很久的神作。

review_english: Elden Ring really belongs to that kind of game that is torturous and unstoppable. When you first start playing, you may be hacked to death by the boss and start to doubt your life. But when you finally win, the feeling of pleasure is really high. Humans may be born to like suffering, otherwise this game would not be so popular. The open world of the game is very large, and it is not that boring. If you wander around, you may find hidden dungeons, strange NPCs, or a monster bigger than a house will suddenly appear and give you a good meal. The whole exploration process is particularly surprising. The combat system is still the classic high-difficulty style of FromSoftware. The operation requires reaction and patience, but every time you successfully dodge and counter-kill the boss, you will feel a special sense of accomplishment. You can also play in different genres, such as wielding a big sword, playing magic long-range bombing, or being an underworld assassin. It is very interesting. The graphics and music are also top-notch, especially during boss battles. When the music plays, the sense of oppression is full. Some scenes are even so cool that you will stop and take screenshots, although you may be killed by a monster sneak attack the next second. The developers obviously have no compassion for the players, but this game is not acceptable to everyone. If you don’t like high difficulty or don’t have the patience to keep dying and starting over, it may be very difficult to play. But if you can accept the challenge, then Elden Ring is definitely a masterpiece that can make people addicted for a long time.

review:
Как бы я не бомбил на элден из-за сложности и пустого открытого мира, но с*ка, какая же эта афигенная игра.
Раньше считал ее вовсе шлаком, и во время прохождения тоже, но после второго босса (годрик) я понял, что эта игра - шедевр.
Игра по большей части отличается от своих предвестников (трилогия дс) из-за опен ворлда, и что ты можешь на время скипнуть босса и в любой момент с ним сразиться. Многие считают это минусом, некоторые вовсе считают это казуальным, но по мне это не так.
Начну наверно с плюсов игры. В этой игре прекрасно абсолютно все; 
сюжет, который вполне себе интересный, в целом весь лор который прописан до мелочей, лвл дизайн локаций, сайд квесты нпс (мой самый любимый с миллисентой, данг итером и с ренной), система прокачки, разнообразия пушек, талисманов и прочей фигни, кастомизация перса, боссы (не учитывается те которые в дандже) и всякая всячина. 
атмосфера у этой игры присутствует, веет неким вайбом берсерка (хотя в целом, элден буквально экранизация берсерка).
я так давно не получал таких эмцоий как от элден ринг, и я даже не жалею что потратил 2к на стим версию, потому что в игре буквально дофига контента которые в общей сложности займет 100+ часов. Сам я наиграл 120+ часов (70 на пиратке, 50 на лицухе) и то не до конца облутал весь мир. 
Из минусов я могу перечислить только пустой опен ворлд и однотипные крипы, на этом все. 
Повторюсь, настоятельно рекомендую к покупке. Эта игра однозначно заслужила игру года, т.к буквально лучший соулслайк эвер, в котором прекрасно абсолютно все.
Моя оценка 10/10. Шедевр от Миядзаки.

review:
This review is more about a very positive impression than a game description. Not necessarily meaningful if you don't know Dark Souls games.

I love the Dark Souls games but I always got stuck at some point with about 3 possible enemies that I couldn't defeat even with reading tips. These games are hard and I guess I'm not good at this.

However, when I saw Elden Ring at a sale I was on the fence but boredom won and I didn't regret it at all. I find it so much better with the much more open world approach. If I get stuck somewhere I go back to easier places. There are so many secrets and hidden things that it's impossible to find all of them at the first or even second go through. Or I sneak around in much harder areas hoping to find better equipment without fighting (and dying).

Another aspect I really like are that you can summon ghosts that fight for you. Even if you are not good at fighting with hectic key presses, studying enemy moves and creating an effective build (all of that applies to me), over time you can upgrade your ghosts and at some point they can defeat bosses for you.

You might wonder why I like playing this if I'm so bad at this. It's the ambience, visuals and strange beings you meet. There is a really beautiful and strange world to explore. I absolutely don't mind revisiting areas  in this game if I'm (or my ghost) to weak to continue. I just enjoying playing it.

review:
8/10 (на самом деле во время прохождения большей части игры я получал настолько огромное от нее удовольствие, что я думал, что это будет одна из любимейших моих игр. И открытый мир интересный (даже с учетом повторок) и боссы занимательные и локации порой просто захватывают дух. Но чем ближе я был к финалу, тем тускнее становились мои чувства. Мувсет боссов начал изобиловать тонной ваншотов и грабатак, локации особо ничем не выделялись и скорее душили, чем развлекали, и вишенкой на торте стали два босса, а именно финальный и Маления, которые высосали из меня всю душу в неприятном смысле слова. Да они сложные, да их убивать было долго и муторно, но самое печальное, после битвы не было катарсиса, была только истощенность. В общем игра стоит того, чтобы ее попробовать и 95% времени я был рад покупке, но финальные этапы конечно изрядно подпортили мне итоговое ощущение)

P.S. ДЛС 2/10 (умудриться сделать все минусы игры еще хуже и вообще не проделать работу над ошибками, это надо еще уметь. Просто душное дополнение, которое высасывает силы и не приносит удовольствия)

review:
I wish I could forget everything and play this game again with fresh eyes.

In 25 years of gaming, I have encountered nothing like it. The game gripped me the moment I stepped into the world - a landscape that is grand, mysterious, and entirely free to explore. 
It refuses to hold your hand. 

I vividly remember my first major boss battle in Stormveil Keep with Margit taking over 30 attempts (later I learned this was entirely optional but recommended). There were many a rage-quit night before the eventual victory...and when he finally fell, the adrenaline and dopamine hit was unlike anything else. I was immediately hooked into the cycle.

60+ hours later, the scale of the world and the technical achievement remain staggering. From the highest peaks to the expansive underworld, the world is packed with puzzles, hidden treasures and unmarked quests. Nothing feels repetitive; no two encounters play out the same way. 

While I’ve stayed strictly in single-player and avoided the save-file corruption issues or invasion fiascos reported by others, my experience has been seamless. However, be aware that these are ongoing issues which I'm not sure are being resolved.

In brief, Elden Ring is a masterpiece of world-building. Developers are already taking notes on how to craft an adventure this immersive, and they should - this game sets a gold standard.

review:
For this being my first souls-like game that I've put time in. I can confidentally say that this has become one of my favourite games. 
1a. The game atmosphere is beautiful, the creatures are so unique in thier own ways and it helps make the game more lively. This also includes main bosses, mini bosses and random hard enemies you encounter.
1b. The different regions you come across is so cool to me. The different affects that might occur as well as the different enemies that are specific to each region.
2. I enjoy the basically free reign running round of an open map, this gives me stuff to explore which is one of my favourite things about open world games and being able to enjoy this while in Elden Ring is so nice. 
3. I've only got 1 boss done; Margit. Yes it was difficult at first, but having a good understanding of movements and attacks really help you get through the boss entirely.

The only issue that I've come across and it might be just me since I'm new to souls-like games is not having that option to see objectives or tasks you are doing. I know listening to the dialoge is supposed to help guide you, and I try my best to sit and listen/read, but it's difficult to have an understanding at some points with where I need to go.

Overall for my first time playing and having 11.8 hours put into it I'll 100% give it a solid 9/10
