review:
Un footjob da Sellen mi cambierebbe la vita.



oltre a questo elden ring è un capolavoro e le motivazioni del perchè sia cosi gia si sanno.
però porcodio alcune cose fanno incazzare, prima di tutto lo sbilanciamento di alcune zone a livello di nemici e bossfight.
per quale cazzo di motivo radahn (senza evocare nessuno degli stronzi) è uno dei boss più FACILI non solo di tutto il gioco ma di caelid in particolare? il dislivello fra lui e il gargoyle prima della bestia è allucinante e senza senso e ti fa solo incazzare farlo first try e poi prendere il cazzo anche per ore dagli altri. stessa cosa succede alla capitale che, mi dispiace dirlo, ma le sentinelle dell albero madre e quella draconica sono assolutamente busted in confronto a morgott (mi dispiace perchè ha forse il miglior moveset del gioco base come boss), e infine quella stracazzo di sentinella draconica prima di maliketh che, per fortuna, skippabile ma non rende meno insensato il fatto che non solo faccia più danni (in seconda fase oneshotta anche con 30 di vitalità se non avete talismani difensivi) ma ABBIA ANCHE PIù VITA DI MALIKETH STESSO cosa che risulterà nel trovare più difficile lui piuttosto che maliketh essendo super squishy e facendo pressoché gli stesso se non meno danni.
seconda cosa il cazzo di dlc che madonna troia non mi capacito di come la qualità si abbassi cosi tanto, e non parlo della bellezza generale del gioco e delle bossfight (bayle rimane insieme al dreglord i boss migliori) ma alla difficoltà (che poi difficoltà non è) della maggioranza delle bossfight.
iniziando il dlc da zero si avrà il livello dei frammenti d ombra a ovviamento zero. questo porterà a trovare le bossfight iniziali le più difficili e frustranti per un motivo pienamente artificiale.
es. se si affronta la bestia divina a livello 3 dei frammenti d ombra non sarà meno difficile di affrontare radahn consorte al livello massimo e questo comporterà a trovare gli stessi boss per il quale hai faticato a bestia all inizio più facili anche fino a ng+ 3. perchè diciamocelo chiaro nemmeno radahn o messmer o anche bayle sono difficili se hai i frammenti al livello 20 e questo distrugge tutta la "difficoltà" che invece si sentiva nel gioco base.
l ultima cosa è il sistema di schivate o in generale di cose che il giocatore può fare per combattere o difendersi dai boss. ragazzi questo non è più darksouls dove i boss hanno 3 mosse in croce, le persone trovavano difficile midir o friede (in elden ring anche margit la prima volta che lo affronti "powercreepa" ogni boss nei precedenti souls) il giocatore dovrebbe avere nuove mosse e possibilità di schivata e anche più moveset e abilità che effettivamente sono forti e aiutano nelle bossfight (la maggioranza di ceneri sono inutili e fanno cagare contro quasi tutte le bossfight se si gioca da soli)
Però dai hanno migliorato le magie peak 10/10

review: 基于我目前的体验，我只能说不推荐，死亡惩罚大，地图探索恶心，赶路久，路上一堆怪，有时候还有特殊地形恶心你，还有一堆阴间路线，可能会不小心摔下悬崖，然后又要重跑，这种路线设计在我目前的游玩中可以说是贯穿始终啊，作者真的贼喜欢这种令人恼怒的设计，今天印象最深刻的就是在魔法学院被一个叫铁处女的玩意儿宰了之后直接跑到火山去，然后赶了一堆路，又碰见两只铁处女，这怪最恶心的就是疯狂抓取，被抓取只能疯狂动你的摇杆，还有你的攻击键来让他对你造成伤害小一点，萌新血短点的可以直接放弃了，动都不用动，直接躺地板上，然后这怪血还厚，火山那两只，一只远程攻击，一只直接突进，近身还得被抓两下，抓到基本就必死，给他除完了，风景不错，特效好看，但是路线实在是恶心，火山那两只没打过之后直接跑回魔法学院，然后又要打那只铁处女，要走那个大风车之后没打过，又要重新走一次，就跟电梯一样，每次都要等起码几十秒吧，在以上我说的期间里一点正反馈都不会给你，只能赶路跟被怪恶心，我在打过魔法学院的那一只之后什么奖励都没有，说真的，能玩的下去这种游戏的，我感觉什么游戏都能玩的下去了

review_english: Based on my current experience, I can only say that I don’t recommend it. The death penalty is huge, the map exploration is disgusting, and the journey is long. There are a lot of monsters on the road. Sometimes there are special terrains that are disgusting. There are also a lot of underworld routes. You may accidentally fall off the cliff and then have to run again. This route design can be said to be consistent throughout my current play. The author really likes this irritating design. The most impressive thing today is that after being killed by a thing called Iron Maiden in the Magic Academy, I ran directly to the volcano, and then after a lot of driving, I met two Iron Maidens. The most disgusting thing about this monster is the crazy grabbing. When caught, you can only move your joystick crazily and your attack button to make it less damage to you. If you are a newbie with a short health, you can just give up and just lie on the floor without moving. Then this monster's health is still thick. For the two volcanoes, one attacks from a distance and the other directly rushes in. If you get close, you will be caught twice. If you catch him, you will basically die. Get rid of him. The scenery is good and the special effects are good, but the route is really disgusting. After the two volcanoes were not beaten, they ran back to the Magic Academy again, and then they had to fight the Iron Maiden again. They had to walk up to the windmill, which they hadn't fought before, and they had to walk again. It was just like the elevator. You had to wait at least tens of seconds every time. During the period I mentioned above, I won't give you any positive feedback. You can only rush and be disgusted by the monsters. I didn't get any rewards after beating the one from the Magic Academy. To be honest, I can play this kind of game. I feel like I can play any game.

review:
作为魂系巅峰之作，《艾尔登法环》将宫崎英高的暗黑美学与开放世界完美融合，当之无愧的年度游戏标杆。广袤辽阔的交界地地图没有空洞填充，从苍翠利耶尼亚湖区、萧瑟宁姆格福荒原，到阴森盖利德腐地、宏伟王城罗德尔，每一片区域地貌、生态、怪物设计截然不同，海量隐藏洞窟、地下永恒之城藏满惊喜，自由探索的乐趣贯穿全程。
承袭经典魂系硬核战斗，高门槛的操作考验反应与耐心，小怪暗藏致命招式，BOSS 战气势磅礴。从巨龙、半神到神祇级敌人，每场鏖战从挫败到破关的成就感无可替代。丰富的职业、武器、法术、战灰构筑海量玩法，近战莽夫、法术法师、灵巧刺客、祷告流信徒随心搭配，同一 BOSS 能衍生数十种通关思路，重复游玩价值拉满。
碎片化叙事依旧是系列特色，没有直白主线，透过道具说明、NPC 支线、场景残骸拼凑破碎的黄金树神话，半神、褪色者、外神的恩怨史诗厚重苍凉，交界地的衰败故事充满宿命美感。零散的支线人物各有悲欢，善恶抉择带来不同结局，沉浸感十足。
从初入交界地屡屡死在大树守卫刀下，到走遍地图揭开世界真相，无数玩家在一次次倒下与再战中成长。游戏兼顾硬核与休闲，新手可召唤骨灰降低难度，硬核玩家能裸装挑战全 BOSS。凭借顶尖的地图设计、战斗体系与世界观塑造，《艾尔登法环》重新定义开放世界，是所有动作、RPG 爱好者不容错过的传世经典。

review_english: As the pinnacle of the Souls series, Elden Ring perfectly integrates Hidetaka Miyazaki's dark aesthetics with the open world and is a well-deserved benchmark for games of the year. The vast border map has no holes to fill, from the green Lienian Lake District, the solitude of Mgofo Wasteland to the gloomy Galede Rot and the magnificent royal city of Rodel. Each region has completely different landforms, ecology, and monster designs. There are tons of hidden caves, and the underground Eternal City is full of surprises. The fun of free exploration runs throughout the process. Inheriting the classic soul-based hard-core combat, high-threshold operations test reaction and patience. The monsters hide hidden deadly moves. The BOSS battle is majestic. From dragons, demigods to god-level enemies, the sense of accomplishment from frustration to victory in each fierce battle is irreplaceable. Rich professions, weapons, spells, battle ashes, massive gameplay, melee reckless man, spell mage, dexterous assassin, prayer flow believer, mix and match as you like. The same BOSS can derive dozens of ways to pass the level. The replay value is full. Fragmented narrative is still a feature of the series. There is no straightforward main line. NPC side lines are explained through props. The broken myth of the golden tree is pieced together from the ruins of the scene. Demigods and Faded Ones. The grudge epic of the outer gods is thick and desolate. The decline story of the junction is full of fatalistic beauty. Scattered side characters have their own joys and sorrows. Good and evil choices bring different endings. Full of immersion. Since I first entered the junction, I have repeatedly died under the knife of the big tree guard. Travel across the map to uncover the truth about the world. Countless players have grown up through falling down and fighting again. The game is both hardcore and casual. Novices can summon ashes to reduce the difficulty. Hardcore players can challenge all BOSSs naked. With top-notch map design, combat system and world view shaping, Elden's Ring redefines the open world. It is a classic that all action RPG enthusiasts cannot miss.

review:
Uno de los mejores juegos que he podido jugar en mucho tiempo, mi primer juego de From y también el primer juego al que me he dedicado a conseguir todos sus logros y "completarlo al 100%" porque realmente este juego nunca termina y siempre hay algo que descubrir, un nuevo lugar que explorar, un boss secreto que derrotar y ahora que tengo su DLC se vienen unas nuevas tierras que recorrer.
Definitivamente un GOTY más que merecido una experiencia que vale totalmente la vivir, ojalá pudieran lavarme el cerebro para poder jugar este juego otra vez y vivirlo como lo hice en su primer día, es simplemente bello en su apartado artístico, te amo Miyazaki gracias por tanto y perdón por tan poco.
Que vivan las patas pero ya deja de poner zonas de veneno.

review:
Elden Ring was my first souls game (on xbox) and it couldnt have been a better introduction into the genre.
My biggest advice to new players is: Dont look up guides on your first or even second playthrough.  Test out spells and weapons that you find and create your own builds. instead of copying a meta build from youtube or tiktok. 

The world is beautiful and full of secrets, the bosses are somewhat challenging and the game is (imo) a a game everyone needs to have experienced once in their life.
10/10.

a beautiful game, with an even more devestatingly beautiful storyline. literally no game will ever compare.
the feeling of finally defeating someone you've spent countless hours on, finding invisible bridges and hidden loot, it all is just so unique and beats any other game ive ever played.
as someone who's mainly played FPS games, I was always hesitant to play a souls game, thinking that the combat was going to be too hard but once you get the hang of it, it comes naturally! just stay calm, and DONT. GIVE. UP.
get this game!!!!!!

review:
[b]Games fun, [i]very casual (non-derogatory)[/i] souls game, open world feels like an afterthought; lots of really generic catacombs, caves and mines that makes Oblivion dungeons feel fun to explore.[/b]

   [b]Few gripes[/b]
[list]
[*] Bosses aside from the main ones are just regular enemies but large and there's a lot of repeats.
[*] Most enemy types will already be encountered before you beat your second main boss.
[*] Boring exploration, as stated before all the dungeons are very dull and primitive, you'll get one or two items out of them, sometimes it'll be good.
[*] Game heavily focuses on using the horse for things but it cannot be upgraded at all, not even cosmetics.
[*] Souls NPC Questlines, if you know you know, now add an entire open-world of sequence skipping on accident.
[*] Crafting mechanics.
[/list]
   [b]Couple Positives[/b]
[list]
[*] Co-op is massively streamlined, you get the ability to touch statues to make it yourself available to be summoned to that location remotely, not only that it adds it to a pool of potential places to be summoned, insane upgrade from having one static summon sign, wish you could use both at the same time though.
[*] Posture from Sekiro, practically all enemies can be staggered from beating the living hell out of them, leaving them exposed to critical finishers.
[*] Heavy attacks matters, in the old games heavy attacks were usually a waste of time since light attacks would out-dps them and leave you a lot less vulnerable, in this, heavy attacks on most enemies will knock'em out of animations so you can pulverize them (can be a negative since it can easily be abused)
[*] Sorceries look a lot cooler, that's pretty all there is to say, they still boil down to press the win button from across the map but they look really rad.
[*] Miracles got a glow up, lots more offensive and cool abilities for priest players
[*] Prop hunt is a re-usable item
[*] Invasions are locked behind co-op, you will never be invaded by a real player unless you're playing co-op. making invading a lot more intense since it'll always be a 1vs2 situation at least (can be a negative especially since the rewards is not all that great at times and the invasion items sparing until you get your hands on the re-usable one)
[*] Crafting mechanics.
[/list]

review:
フロム卒業の作品になるかも。

良い所
世界観
フィールドの雰囲気の良さは好きですね。ぱっと開けた世界にわくわくするようなオブジェクトがはるか遠くに見える。OWのフィールドはこうあってほしいものです。
キャラや設定も焼き直し感はあるものの過去作よりとっ付きやすくなってて好印象です。

派手目になった戦闘
ダクソなどは何使っても大して変わらんなって感じだったが、今作は結構特色が出て楽しかった。
ステ振りも直しも完全自由ではないが普通にやる分には制限気にせず変えれたので。

悪い所
戦闘そのもの
これがあまりにもしょーもなさ過ぎて、次回作は様子見かな。
ダクソとかだったら攻撃をよく見切らないと無駄ローリングで攻撃チャンスが潰れるという、リターンの減少で回避の最適化にインセンティブを与えていました。
今作はそもそもローリングを狩って殺すことしか考えていません。
そうするとそもそもの試行チャンスが少なくなり、異常な火力と合わさりワンミスですべてがおじゃんという徒労感を与えているだけです。
よく言われるディレイもフェイントのようなモーションを何段もかけた上でやってくるので感覚的によけると馬鹿を見る、常に騙し打ちのような攻撃しかしてきません。
1ボスに一つとかなら理解できますが、そのような攻撃しかないので攻撃振られるだけで非常にストレスです。
感覚的に8割くらいは避けれる攻撃だしてプレイヤーよいしょしたらどうですか？
ステージの奥に行ってこのだまし討ちディレイでワンパン即死すると悔しさより真顔になります。
またこの力み切ったモーションから繰り出される超範囲攻撃をいなしたと思って攻撃をします。
さぞあれだけの大技当然隙がでかいでしょと。いいえ、そこをキャンセルして攻撃してきますしかも全方位。
モーションの不快さ、攻撃と硬直のバランス。ゲーム自体に納得感が微塵も感じられません。
負けるときは超火力、超範囲攻撃、あまりにも不釣り合いな攻撃動作と硬直、だまし討ちに疲弊しながら死んでいく。
勝つ時は超戦技連発やダウン速攻ではい終わり。
ストレスだけ爆増させて、達成感をスポイルした最悪の方向に舵を切ったなと思いました。
死にゲーｷｬｯｷｬｯ

とは言えそれ以外はクオリティは高いし楽しいので、最初から強戦法予習して蹂躙して遊ぶならおすすめです。

review_english: It might be a work that graduates from From.Good points Worldview I like the good atmosphere of the field.You can see exciting objects far away in the world that opens up.This is what I want the field of OW to be.Although the characters and settings feel like a rehash, I have a good impression that they are easier to get used to than the previous works.The flashy battles I felt that no matter what I used with Daxo etc., it didn't change much, but this work has a lot of distinctive features and was fun I wasn't completely free to move and modify my stats, but I was able to change them without worrying about restrictions as I normally do them.Bad points: The combat itself was too boring, so I'll have to wait and see in the next game.If it was Daxo or something like that, if you don't judge the attack well, you'll lose your chance to attack due to wasted rolling.The reduction in returns gave an incentive to optimize your evasion.In this game, all you're thinking about is hunting and killing rolling.If you do that, you'll have fewer chances to try in the first place. Combined with the abnormal firepower, it just gives the feeling that everything is a waste of time if you make a single mistake. The delay, which is often said, comes after applying a number of feint-like motions, so if you dodge it intuitively, you will look foolish. It will always only attack like a deception attack. I can understand if there is only one attack per boss, but since there is only one attack like that, it is very stressful just to be attacked. How about making the player feel that the attack is one that can be dodged intuitively about 80% of the time? If you go to the back of the stage and instantly die with one punch with this deceptive attack delay, your face will be more serious than frustration.Also, you will attack, thinking that you have dodged the super range attack that is unleashed from this powerful motion.Of course, there will be a huge gap with such a big move. The attack movements and stiffness are so disproportionate, I die while being exhausted from the deceitful attacks, and when I win, I just end up using a series of super combat moves or knockdown quick attacks.I think the game has taken a turn in the worst possible direction, only increasing stress and spoiling the sense of accomplishment.The death game is crazy.However, other than that, the quality is high and it's fun. Recommended if you want to prepare for strong tactics from the beginning and play by overrunning.

review: I honestly don’t even know what to say, it is absolute PERFECTION. I’ve poured hours and days into this game, and I don't regret a single second of it. What an incredible open-world design! Just when you're heading somewhere on the map, a cave you stumble upon can lead you into a whole different underground world. The boss designs, the soundtrack, the combat impact, the absolute freedom... That flawless formula that makes you say 'just one more try' instead of getting tilted when you die has reached its absolute peak here. If you still haven’t played it, do yourself a favor and do not miss out on this experience. Though, I hope you have divine patience before starting this game. Good luck to everyone! 10/10!

review: Jogos da From são celebrados, acima de tudo, por conta de seus chefes desafiadores e memoráveis. Você não vai encontrar isso em Elden Ring; apesar de o jogo ter mais chefes do que grão de areia na praia, a maioria deles pode ser derrotado por um portador de Alzheimer com histórico de derrames. Até os chefes mais glorificados não chegam perto do xadrez que é um duelo pvp qualquer de Dark Souls 2. Dito isso, é o meu jogo favorito da empresa, onde a soma de seus piores defeitos não supera suas maiores qualidades. A jornada é cativante, os cenários são inspiradores e a exploração, mesmo que repetitiva, fórmulaica e maçante, não deixa de ser recompensadora, te dando novas formas de construir seu personagem e ferramentas para explorar o verdadeiro brilho do sistema de combate presente nessa aventura. Recomendação instantânea, não como o sucessor da série Souls, mas como um excelente jogo de aventura.

review:
Good game! Great visuals, great combat, insane level of flexibility to the point that 60% of the game can be considered optional if you're good enough. Loot progression is immensely satisfying, the game make sures to make every mechanic, consumable etc useful enough that nothing feels useless. Huge replay value too, I finished the game on a strength/faith build but am already excited to try again focusing on a mage build, the freedom is crazy.

Legacy dungeons were easily my favourite part about the game, lots of hidden shortcuts and treasures with fun progression through various wild bosses, the game really encourages you to explore the world without it feeling like a constant grind. Most of all though the sheer scale of imagination was astounding, beautiful underground cities and massive mega castles  on mountaintops etc, starry night skies, cool autumn vibes, theres even a completely optional part of the map thats in a constant blizzard which is really surreal.

First time fromsoft player and its easily a solid 9/10, challenging boss fights can be made much easier by exploring the world and finding better loot etc and then coming back, to the point of sometimes nerfing yourself just to feel more challenged. My only pet peeve was the inconsistent narrative structure, the worldbuilding was incredible I just wish they utilised cutscnes more and that side quests didn't feel so cryptic sometimes. You'll have to pull up online wikis to backtrack steps and progress side quests which gets annoying but you'll get used to it, good thing is theres load of good guides online anytime you feel lost. Anyways hope you enjoy!

review:
You know, I spent so much time playing, I entirely forgot to write a review.

This game's brilliant. More than brilliant, honestly. I've played the Souls games, I'm familiar with the formula, and I feel like this game does it the best. Every build is enjoyable and rewarding in its own way - even playing as a base Wretch isn't all that awful, given the awesome r1 combos you can do with the club.

The art direction is more evolved than any Souls game I've ever played, to be honest. It knows what it wants to look like at all times and it literally never breaks that view. City design, boss design, and weapon design leave *nothing* to be desired. 

The story's great, too. I mean, it's a Souls story, so you can't expect the game to read like a Dr. Seuss novel, but once you can wrap your head around the world and its characters it's genuinely an incredibly enjoyable setting.

Honestly, this game's just great. One of the best. Exploration, gameplay, art, music, all that. You'll like it.

10/10

review:
Primeiro jogo da fromsoftware que eu zero meu contato com os anteriores foram, Demon souls PS3, Darksouls 2, Wolong e sekiro, com exceção do Sekiro todos eu achei uma grande porcaria, e sempre meu maior problema com os jogos dessa empresa é na questão de level design posicionamento de mobs e danos incomuns você upa upa upa e parece que fica do mesmo jeito inimigo tankando e você levando danos absurdos

Mas falando de Elden Ring em si, mundo aberto tem bastante coisas para fazer se você tiver tempo sobrando vale a pena explorar bastante...

Eu joguei de guerreiro sem usar summons tirando 3 bosses de final de jogo, Reykard, Maliketh, e o Radagon e esse jogo te pune absurdamente se você não for mago e não usar os recursos que estão no jogo claro que se você for aquele tipo de pessoa que tem habilidades incomuns de desviar de tudo ai tudo bem kk mas se você for um player comum vai ser um pouco sofrido pelo menos no final do jogo

O Jogo em si é muito fácil e é o que eu sempre questiono sobre a fromsoftware o pessoal pensa que dano alto dos bosses e mobs torna o jogo difícil mas não apenas torna o jogo desagradável em alguns lugares

repete muitos inimigos chefes como mobs comuns isso vale ponto negativo, tirando os principais.

O gráfico é muito bonito pra um jogo tão grande assim parabéns apesar do anti cheat estragar muito a performance do jogo eu fiz o teste sem anti cheat 60fps cravado, com anti cheat ativo o tempo todo 40 50 e oscilando

Design dos bosses muito top realmente são imponentes tirando a renalla que boss patético e preguiçoso, mas tem uma lore legal

A primeira fase do radagon é masterpiece gostei bastante, ja a segunda fase kkk.....

Minha nota pra esse jogo é um 6/10

review_english: The first fromsoftware game that I had no contact with, the previous ones were Demon souls PS3, Darksouls 2, Wolong and sekiro, with the exception of Sekiro I all found it to be a big piece of rubbish, and always my biggest problem with the games from this company is the issue of level design, mob positioning and unusual damage, you level up and it seems like the enemy tanks the same way and you take absurd damage. But speaking of Elden Ring itself, the open world has a lot of things to do if you have the time. the rest is worth exploring a lot... I played as a warrior without using summons except for 3 end-game bosses, Reykard, Maliketh, and Radagon and this game punishes you absurdly if you're not a wizard and don't use the resources that are in the game, of course if you're that type of person who has unusual abilities to dodge everything then that's fine lol but if you're a regular player it will be a bit of a struggle at least at the end of the game The game itself is very easy and that's what I always question about the fromsoftware people think that high damage from bosses and mobs makes the game difficult but not only does it make the game unpleasant in some places it repeats many boss enemies as well as common mobs this is worth a negative point, apart from the main ones. review:
The first time I played this game I got to the first real batch of enemies and died so many times I uninstalled and refunded the game. About a year later I thought I'd give it another shot. Now I've put over 300 hours into the game, and I'd say it's one of my favorite games of all time. 

This was my first Souls game, and it took some getting used to. With a beginner's guide and the right mindset, you too can learn to embrace the pain and frustration and learn to love it.

Elden Ring, due to the open world, basically allows you to choose your difficulty. You can over-level for every single boss if you want. Or you can run to the bosses with no gear and try to kill them without getting hit. It's up to you.
 
This game, more than almost any other I've played, rewards you for everything you learn. Every time you die, you get a little better, and that is incredibly satisfying. 

The only thing I dislike and could say is a flaw is the platforming. There are times when the geometry will move you or you might not jump as far as you think. Just accept that you'll have stupid deaths from falling off ledges. 

I highly recommend this game to others who aren't sure about Souls games and would like to try them out.

review:
Оригинальный Элден - это, пожалуй, лучшая игра Миядзаки для меня (до этого я прошёл всю серию Тёмных Душ и Секиро, так что в жанре я далеко не новичок).

Мир Междуземья мне очень понравился. Многие называют его пустым, но я не согласен: здесь есть чем заняться, а исследовать его интересно. Да, находить ненужные прахи или пульсирующие листы это такое себе, но часто игра вознаграждает хорошим оружием, броней, талисманом или заклинанием (даже если лут не подходит под твой класс, все равно приятно).

Квесты персонажей считаю чуть ли не лучшим, что есть в игре. Да, система взаимодействия с ними кажется сильно устаревшей, но проработка почти всех героев отличная: их запоминаешь и к ним привязываешься. Некоторые квесты (например, сюжет Ранни) это вообще абсолют синема, такого Миядзаки раньше точно не делал.

С врагами тоже все хорошо. Многие жалуются на повторы, но типов противников в игре огромное множество, поэтому они распределены так, что это почти не раздражают. Часто уже знакомые враги получают новые способности или оружие, что разнообразит схватки и не дает игрокам душиться.

Боевую систему я считаю эталоном жанра соулслайков. Добавление таких механик, как пепел войны, контратака из блока и прыжок, заметно разнообразит геймплей и часто упрощает сражения. Если бы игроки чаще использовали эти возможности во время битв, особенно с боссами, жалоб на последних было бы куда меньше.

Кстати о боссах. Многие их ругают, но лично мне эта критика кажется совершенно незаслуженной. Настолько выдающихся сражений не делали ни Фромы, ни кто-либо другой. Конечно, те же Маления, Мог или Маликет гораздо сложнее большинства боссов из прошлых игр, но и у игроков появилось немало новых возможностей. И я говорю не о Ложной Слезе, которую "хардкорщики" любят приводить в пример как единственную механику, способную облегчить прохождение. Те же прыжки или грамотное позиционирование в разы упрощают многие схватки, особенно в ДЛС.

Локации, особенно сюжетные, здесь великолепны. Ничего подобного Замку Грозовой Завесы, Лейнделлу (и катакомбам под ним), Фарум-Азуле или Пристанищу Теней вы ни в одной другой игре не встретите - эти места невероятно хороши. Открытый мир это уже дело вкуса: кому-то, как мне, нравится заглядывать под каждый камень и исследовать каждые руины, а кому-то нет. При этом такой контент необязателен, а важные пещеры, в которых находятся сферы для покупки кузнечных камней, отмечены на карте.

Единственное, что можно было бы улучшить, как мне кажется, это прогрессию по регионам. Полная свобода перемещения по миру может привести к тому, что вы либо пропустите квест персонажа, либо будете ощущать себя недокачанным или, наоборот, перекачанным. Думаю, Фромам стоило закрыть некоторые локации до выполнения определённых условий. Зачатки этой системы в игре уже есть: например, подъёмники Дектуса и Рольда, но этого недостаточно. Поэтому тем, кто только собирается начать играть, я бы посоветовал использовать карту прогрессии: так вы лучше почувствуете развитие персонажа и с меньшей вероятностью пропустите важные квесты.

Что касается ДЛС, оно понравилось мне меньше оригинальной игры, хотя всё равно получилось очень хорошим. Мне понравились новые персонажи и их взаимосвязанный квест, уменьшенное количество данжей с большим упором на их уникальность, новые типы оружия и почти все боссы, кроме финального. Не понравились обилие пустых локаций и недостаток новых противников, большинство из них перекочевало из оригинальной игры. Тем не менее сражения с Реланной, Мессмером и Мидрой, а также интересные квесты и лор всё равно стоят того, чтобы пройти дополнение. Кстати, в осколках тоже никакой проблемы не вижу, нормальная прогрессия.

Оригинальной игре я бы поставил 9,5/10, а дополнению — 8/10.

Многие их ругают, но лично мне эта критика кажется совершенно незаслуженной. Настолько выдающихся сражений не делали ни Фромы, ни кто-либо другой. Конечно, те же Маления, Мог или Маликет гораздо сложнее большинства боссов из прошлых игр, но и у игроков появилось немало новых возможностей. И я говорю не о Ложной Слезе, которую "хардкорщики" любят приводить в пример как единственную механику, способную облегчить прохождение. Те же прыжки или грамотное позиционирование в разы упрощают многие схватки, особенно в ДЛС. Локации, особенно сюжетные, здесь великолепны. Ничего подобного Замку Грозовой Завесы, Лейнделлу (и катакомбам под ним), Фарум-Азуле или Пристанищу Теней вы ни в одной другой игре не встретите - эти места невероятно хороши. Открытый мир это уже дело вкуса кому-то, как мне, нравится заглядывать под каждый камень и исследовать каждые руины, а кому-то нет. При этом такой контент необязателен, а важные пещеры, в которых находятся сферы для покупки кузнечных камней, отмечены на карте. Единственное, что можно было бы улучшить, как мне кажется, это прогрессию по регионам. Полная свобода перемещения по миру может привести к тому, что вы либо пропустите квест персонажа, либо будете ощущать себя недокачанным или, наоборот, перекачанным. Думаю, Фромам стоило закрыть некоторые локации до выполнения определённых условий. Зачатки этой системы в игре уже есть например, подъёмники Дектуса и Рольда, но этого недостаточно. Поэтому тем, кто только собирается начать играть, я бы посоветовал использовать карту прогрессии так вы лучше почувствуете развитие персонажа и с меньшей вероятностью пропустите важные квесты. Что касается ДЛС, оно понравилось мне меньше оригинальной игры, хотя всё равно получилось очень хорошим. Мне понравились новые персонажи и их взаимосвязанный квест, уменьшенное количество данжей с большим упором на их уникальность, новые типы оружия и почти все боссы, кроме финального. Не понравились обилие пустых локаций и недостаток новых противников, большинство из них перекочевало из оригинальной игры. Тем не менее сражения с Реланной, Мессмером и Мидрой, а также интересные квесты и лор всё равно стоят того, чтобы пройти дополнение. Кстати, в осколках тоже никакой проблемы не вижу, нормальная прогрессия. Оригинальной игре я бы поставил 9,5 10, а дополнению 8 10.

Купил её где-то недели три назад и уже полностью закрыл на все достижения, что можно сказать:
1)Наверное самая лёгкая соулс игра в которую довелось поиграть, до этого были DS2,DS3 и Lies of P,из которых я прошёл только DS2
2)Не понятно нужен ли тут открытый мир, когда закрыл все достижения устал бегать от одного места к другому по однотипным локациям с повторяющимися боссами, может стоило сделать карту меньше чтоб не использовать повторы?
3)Казуалил жёстко, если при прохождении того же DS2 можно было играть за мага чтоб облегчить свои страдания (проходил за война), то тут же можно использовать прахи, которые могут иногда в соло закрыть  босса, говорю конкретно про ложную слезу с бафами хила, про мага вообще молчу-на третье прохождение решил попробовать сыграть с магией-игра прошлась за 4-5 часов (убивая только основных боссов)
4)Наверное лучший вариант для тех кто никогда не пробовал соулс игры, как минимум потому что за счёт открытого мира если ты не можешь победить босса, ты можешь пойти перекачаться на второстепенных боссах и вернуться потом на основного
В общем мне понравилось, оно того стоило)

Overall, I liked it, it was worth it)

review:
Чтож, я прошел Elden Ring не один раз и смело могу заявить, что это одна из лучших игр, в которые я играл.
Сама по себе елда переполнена контентом, множество боссов, билдов, локаций и сюжетных линий. Игру я бы не назвал прям очень сложной, но на паре боссов мое очко реально подгорало. Тут есть много минусов и много плюсов о которых дальше пойдет речь:
Начнем с плюсов:

1. Обширный лор, в который реально интересно погружаться.
Читая разные записки или описания предметов, слушая разные диалоги и проходя сюжет можно понять, насколько Бабадзаки старался наполнить мир

2. Гибкость и вариативность билдов.
В игре можно делать самые разные билды: ты можешь пробегать с 1 палкой всю игру убивая боссов на скиле или собрать самый казуальный магический билд, чтобы шотить каждого босса. Делать косплеи на разных персонажей из других вселенных и проходить игру за них. Все зависит лишь от твоей фантазии

3. Открытый мир.
Сам мир переполнен контентом и красотами. Его интересно как и исследовать, так и просто гулять смотря на местные красоты. Даже проведя в игре сотни часов, можно найти на карте что-то новое, пещеру, поле, босса.

4. Сюжет
Сюжет в игре очень поверхностный на первый взгляд (как и во всех соулс лайках на самом деле) ты - погасший и тебе нужно добраться до Кольца Элден и восстановить, вроде все просто. Но если в сюжет погрузиться сильнее то можно проникнуться им, и ближе к финалу реально всплакнуть.
На самом деле плюсов больше, но это самые основные. Спойлеров давать не буду :D

А теперь к минусам:
1. Порог входа для игры явно не подходит для новичков.
Если в тех же 3-их душах было понятно что тебе делать и куда идти, то в кольце в начале открыт весь мир, ты можешь пробежать пол карты и не понять куда именно тебе идти. Без гайдов и интерактивной карты прохождение получится очень сложным и долгим.

2. Боссы
Обычно во время битвы с боссами на экране происходит полный ♥♥♥♥♥♥. Жирная мразь летает по всей арене спамя в тебя гавном, лишь бы ты ловил идеальные тайминги для переката (Консорт Радан тому пример). Боссы жирные с огромным уроном лишь бы растянуть файт на множество траев. Если сравнивать с Dark Souls 3, то боссы там не имели слишком много хп и слишком много урона. Сами по себе они были балансными и игра не гналась за какой-то ебанутой сложностью.

3. Баги, абузы, скипы
В игре с момента релиза было пофикшено множество багов, например больше нельзя фармить по 300к рун за 10 секунд или чизить множество боссов (зато можно скипнуть финального). Но множество скипов осталось.

Вот и конец моего обзора, хочу сказать, что мнение субъективное и я не считаю его правильным или не правильным, каждый может посчитать из перечисленного минус плюсом или наоборот.
Сама игра отличная, дополнение тоже советую пройти всем, даже тем, кто далек от Душ.
Игре ставлю 8/10
(Поставил бы максимум, но на Эльфаэле и Фарум-Азуле мое очко дико сгорало и я даже дропал игру)

review_english: Well, I've played through Elden Ring more than once and I can safely say that it's one of the best games I've ever played. The game itself is overflowing with content, with many bosses, builds, locations and storylines. I wouldn’t call the game very difficult, but on a couple of bosses my point really burned out. There are many disadvantages and many advantages, which will be discussed further. Let's start with the advantages 1. Extensive lore, which is really interesting to dive into. By reading different notes or descriptions of objects, listening to different dialogues and going through the plot, you can understand how much Babadzaki tried to fill the world of 2. Flexibility and variability of builds. In the game you can make a variety of builds; you can run through the entire game with 1 stick, killing bosses with a skill, or put together the most casual magic build to shoot every boss. Make cosplays of different characters from other universes and complete the game for them. It all depends only on your imagination 3. Open world. The world itself is overflowing with content and beauty. It’s interesting both to explore it and to just walk around looking at the local beauty. Even after spending hundreds of hours in the game, you can find something new on the map, a cave, a field, a boss. 4. Plot The plot of the game is very superficial at first glance (as in all souls likes in fact) you are extinguished and you need to get to the Ring of Elden and restore it, everything seems simple. But if you immerse yourself more deeply in the plot, you can become imbued with it, and closer to the end you will actually cry. In fact, there are more advantages, but these are the most basic. I won’t give any spoilers D And now to the disadvantages 1. The entry threshold for the game is clearly not suitable for beginners. If in the same 3 souls it was clear what you should do and where to go, then in the ring at the beginning the whole world is open, you can run through half the map and not understand where exactly you should go. Without guides and an interactive map, the passage will be very difficult and long. 2. Bosses Usually, during a boss battle, the screen is full of . Fat scum flies all over the arena spamming shit at you, as long as you catch the perfect timing for a roll (Consort Radan is an example of this). The bosses are fat with huge damage just to stretch out the fight into many fights. Compared to Dark Souls 3, the bosses there didn't have too much HP or too much damage. By themselves, they were balanced and the game did not chase some kind of fucking complexity. 3. Bugs, abuses, skips Many bugs have been fixed in the game since its release, for example, you can no longer farm 300k runes in 10 seconds or cheat many bosses (but you can skip the final one). But many skips remain. This is the end of my review, I want to say that the opinion is subjective and I do not consider it right or wrong, everyone can consider any of the above a minus as a plus or vice versa. The game itself is excellent, I also advise everyone to play the expansion, even those who are far from Souls. I give the game 8 10 (I would have given the maximum, but on Elfael and Farum-Azul my point burned wildly and I even dropped the game)

review:
OK!

Não me pegou de primeira. Nem de segunda. Nem de quinta.

Mas agora... PQP, que jogo bom.

Depois que passa daquela fase em que qualquer inimigo te mata com um espirro e você deixa de ser um saco de batatas ambulante, o jogo começa a ficar absurdo de bom.

A ambientação e a direção de arte, pra mim, estão facilmente entre as melhores que já vi em um jogo. Não tem informação nenhuma dizendo pra onde ir, o que fazer ou qual caminho seguir, mas quando você começa a sobreviver mais de 30 segundos, isso vira justamente parte da graça.

Você simplesmente escolhe uma direção e vai. Sempre acaba encontrando alguma coisa incrível, uma dungeon escondida, um inimigo bizarro ou um boss que te faz questionar suas escolhas de vida por algumas horas.

Demorou, mas a cocaína da FromSoftware finalmente me pegou.
review_clean: OK! Não me pegou de primeira. Nem de segunda. Nem de quinta. Mas agora... PQP, que jogo bom. Depois que passa daquela fase em que qualquer inimigo te mata com um espirro e você deixa de ser um saco de batatas ambulante, o jogo começa a ficar absurdo de bom. A ambientação e a direção de arte, pra mim, estão facilmente entre as melhores que já vi em um jogo. Não tem informação nenhuma dizendo pra onde ir, o que fazer ou qual caminho seguir, mas quando você começa a sobreviver mais de 30 segundos, isso vira justamente parte da graça. Você simplesmente escolhe uma direção e vai. Sempre acaba encontrando alguma coisa incrível, uma dungeon escondida, um inimigo bizarro ou um boss que te faz questionar suas escolhas de vida por algumas horas. Demorou, mas a cocaína da FromSoftware finalmente me pegou.
review_english: OK! It didn't catch me at first. Not even second-rate. Not even Thursday. But now... PQP, what a good game. Once you get past that stage where any enemy kills you with a sneeze and you stop being a walking sack of potatoes, the game starts to get absurdly good. The setting and art direction, for me, are easily among the best I've ever seen in a game. There's no information telling you where to go, what to do or which path to take, but when you start surviving for more than 30 seconds, that becomes part of the fun. You simply choose a direction and go. You always end up finding something incredible, a hidden dungeon, a bizarre enemy or a boss that makes you question your life choices for a few hours. It took a while, but FromSoftware's cocaine finally got me.

review:
第一次玩魂游，早就听闻艾尔登法环之名，但是选择它其实是因为另一个真正想玩的游戏更难，需要一定基础，真是抱歉了老头环：）
刚打开法环的第一天，我连锁怪都不知道，wasd和shift都按不顺，更不要说用战技，被小兵打死是常有的事，出门被大树守卫狠狠爆金币，被怪追根本不敢回头，交界地很美丽，但是好危险，我真的能玩下去吗？
几十个小时过去了，宁姆格福跑遍了，摩恩城登上了，噩兆妖鬼打过了，史东威尔城清完了，也成功从小葛手里拿到了我的第一个大卢恩，终于是来到湖之利耶尼亚，也走到了盖利德，乘坐大升降台来到了亚坛高原，见到了曾因为传送陷阱窥见一角被狠狠震撼的王城罗德尔。
前有绝景，敬请见证。
迄今为止，我死亡了179次，丢掉的卢恩数不胜数，同时我也是个犟人，打不死boss就一直打，总能打过去，翻滚闪避也逐渐熟练，被坑多了也学会卡视角偷看两边有没有埋伏，法环是恐怖游戏谁赞同谁反对。
我知道我探索的进程可能还不到三分之一，故事主线也才刚刚起步，但是已经感受到法环的魅力所在，诡诞又怪异，吸引着想知道真相的所有人。我好像知道为什么人会逐渐爱上端游然后再也玩不下去手游了哈哈，品鉴这种细糠之后确实很难再去吃浮躁的屎，以前我觉得这是纯嘉豪行为，sorry
等我真正探索完交界地大概还会再写一次测评，困难总会遇到，但是还是那句从游戏初就告诉我们的话。
即使引导早已破碎，也请您当上艾尔登之王。

review_english: It's my first time to play Soul Games. Being beaten to death by minions is a common thing. When I went out, I was heavily guarded by big trees and exploded with gold coins. I didn't dare to look back when chased by monsters. The border area is beautiful but so dangerous. Can I really continue playing? Dozens of hours have passed. I ran around Nimgfu, climbed up Morn City, defeated the Omen Monster, cleared Stonewell City, and successfully got my first big rune from Xiaoge. Finally, I came to Lienia of the Lake, and also walked to Galid. I took the big lift to the Yatan Plateau, and saw the royal city Rodel that was shocked because of a glimpse of the teleportation trap. There is a wonderful view in front of me. Please bear witness. So far, I have died 179 times. I have lost countless runes. At the same time, I am also a stubborn person. I keep fighting the boss if I can't kill it. I can always defeat the boss. I have gradually become proficient in rolling and dodging. After I have been tricked a lot, I have learned to freeze the perspective to see if there are any ambushes on both sides. Who agrees and who disagrees with the fact that Ring of Magic is a horror game. I know that my exploration process is probably less than one-third of the way. The main story line has only just begun, but I have already felt the charm of Ring of Magic. It is weird and weird, attracting everyone who wants to know the truth. I seem to know why people gradually fall in love with high-end games and then can no longer play mobile games. Haha, after tasting this kind of fine chaff, it is really difficult to eat impetuous shit. I used to think this was pure heroic behavior. Sorry, I will probably write another review after I have truly explored the junction. Difficulties will always be encountered, but it is still the same sentence that has been told to us since the beginning of the game. Even if the guidance has long been broken, please be the king of Elden.

review: Fun early game! Really scratches the itch of growing stronger and wrecking previous enemies that the old Monster Hunter games had. It does drag in the mid game though with minimal weapon and level progression. Game actively discourages switching weapons with how limited the upgrade items are. Also, interesting how strangely slow and telegraphed enemies are. But that is something you get used to fast.

review_english: I really don’t know how to make the DLC have high values and pile monsters together. When I first entered the DLC, a wild dog on the roadside chewed me up twice. At each door, there were elites with similar values to the main BOSS guarding the door. After defeating one, there was another one. If you died, you had to run over and fight it again from the beginning. The BOSS was even more difficult to defend. The desire to attack was so high that there was no chance to drink medicine. The special effects were flashy. The values were so exaggerated that the BOSS died without even knowing what it looked like. The map is empty and devoid of anything. Exploring is just like going shopping. The whole thing is like a half-finished product that has been rushed into work.

review:
bon. je vais parler franchement, en répondant également aux problématiques qui poussent certains joueurs à lui mettre un avis négatif.

ce jeu est très crypté. pour le comprendre, il faut regarder des vidéos youtube et lire les descriptions des cendres de guerre, armes, armures et autres objets.  le scénario est fait par Georges R. R. Martin (scénariste de games of throne), il est incroyable. c'est pas les joueurs qui ont passé 30h dessus qui pourront se faire un avis là dessus.

ensuite, le jeu est très CPU dépendant. vous étonnez pas d'avoir des micro freeze avec une RTX 5090 si votre CPU est un ryzen 3. perso j'utilise les graphiques intégrés d'un ryzen 7 5700G et j'ai aucun problème.
pour ceux qui aiment pas être délaissés : c'est un jeu fromsoft. il va pas vous tenir par la main, et il n'y a pas non plus de système d'accompagnement de quêtes (je recommande à ceux qui se retrouvent perdus de tenir un petit journal au lieu d'aller se plaindre dans les comms.). cependant, je peux accepter ceux qui râlent sur la fait que si on tue un seul pnj, on peut ruiner une quête (ou du moins, ne pas y avoir accès au début mais seulement après une zone cachée, comme varre que j'ai buté et j'ai du attendre les champs de neige consacrés pour atteindre le palais de moghwyn)

l'ia : c'est un jeu ou vous êtes censés taper. vous étonnez pas si l'ia est claqué si vous voulez passer en furtivité.

après y'a des points négatifs hein : les boss recyclés qu'on croise dans 30 arènes différentes puis derrière un mur dans une salle random plus tard, la magie trop cheatée, le pvp ou si t'es le doigt crochu tu te retrouve dans une chasse 3v1 ou le mec niv 1 amène ses potes cheaters ou maxeurs (on vous voit sur la falaise des albinauriques petits coquins) niv 900 et tu te retrouves atomisé.

je recommande si vous voulez surtout jouer seul et que vous êtes persistants (genre les gens qui aiment pas attendre pour comprendre dégagez immédiatement)

review:
Elden Ring was my first Soulsbourne game/Fromsoft game back on the PS5 and I have 1500 plus hours on it.  I loved the game so much that I bought it again on PC once I had a good gaming rig.  Because of this game, I recently went back and played all the Dark Souls games and enjoyed the hell out of them as well, so that's another reason I decided to pick this one up again.  

After playing those, I have some different thoughts and insights on this game.  Either way, I suppose it might be helpful to break this down into a Pros/Cons list, so here we go:

Pros:   
-Visually, this is the most beautiful game I've ever played.  I wish it had a legit photo mode because I've taken so many screenshots, it's not funny.  Unfortunately, most of those have health bars or something in them.  The views often look like a painting and are truly a work of art.  And, no, it's just the skyboxes, it's the architecture, overall building design, and locales as well.  

-Best exploration of any open world game I've played - there aren't any icons or checklists, you just explore, get some visual storytelling, and you are frequently rewarded with cool items, lore, and/or visuals.  I played FF VII Rebirth after this and as fun as most of the game is, the open world was... mostly unpleasant.  I wish they could have learned from this game.

-Weapons, armor, and items - oh my.  This really creates a big part of the replayability of this game as you can come up with so many new "builds" and new ways to play. 
-Replayability - Continued from above, but yeah... it's hugely replayable between getting different endings, finishing up questlines you missed, and just being able to play through it completely differently.  The latter was my favorite part as I played through the first time with a Dex/Bleed build and had several struggles toward the end, but when I played as sorcerer I breezed through some of those areas and struggled in different places.  

-Challenging, but rewarding (most of the time) - At first, the combat was painful for me.  I had never played these games before and it just wasn't clicking.  It took way longer than it should have and I tried to play it like a Skyrim stealth archer, so you can imagine how that went.  Still, once it began to click, it became more fun. When you do overcome an obstacle or a tough boss, you feel good about it.
-Spirit Ash Summons - I'm so glad these exist.  I'm currently trying to play without them, unless it's like a reused boss or something just to add some variety, but these helped so much on my first few runs since this was all new to me.  I'm not sure if I would have finished the game without having them as an option.  Of course, it's pretty clear after playing the other 3 games that the bosses here were designed with their use in mind.

Cons:

-Most NPC questlines are borderline impossible without guides - This goes for all of the Souls games, but this one the most.  I completed some of them by accident, but for most of them, I didn't even realize they were quests.  I don't think we need a detailed quest system or anything, but a basic journal would be great. I played Shadows of the Erdtree with some hand-written notes and it helped, but this game is just too big to remember all these NPCs, what they want, and where they're going.  Well, you never know where they're going...

-Endgame difficult spike - Mountaintops of the Giants and Consecrated Snowfields are just unpleasantly difficult. Part of it is the enemies and bosses just have huge health bars, but also they hit ridiculously hard even at high levels.  In addition, some of the catacombs are almost a joke with their difficulty such as when they include some of the difficult early catacomb bosses as regular enemies throughout.  I found myself getting frustrated a lot and in subsequent playthroughs, I skipped a lot in these areas unless I'm looking for specific items.

-Reused bosses - I noticed this more after playing the DS games and coming back to ER, but it is just not fun running into your 4th, 5th, or 10th Erdtree Avatar, Ulcerated Tree Spirit, or Crucible Knight.  I hate it even worse when they just become a regular enemy.  It's not quite as bad if they don't respawn, but it's still just boring.  Shadow of the Erdtree did this the worst with the Ulcerated Tree Spirits, especially in one of the coolest areas of the game.  It's like the only thing that changes is that they're stronger and hit harder.

-Shadows of the Erdtree empty areas - This expansion is beautiful, challenging, and a game in and of itself, but there are some big, empty areas.  I get that some are important for the lore, but I'm not sure why they couldn't have cut some of these down and/or added some other items worth finding.  The Finger ruins, the grassy areas outside of Shadow Keep, and the area with the red flowers are some of the worst offenders.  Again, at least they're all really cool looking, but I got an empty feeling quite often after exploring them.  I found myself asking, "Is that it?" quite often.

-Shadows of the Erdtree final bosses and overall ending to the DLC:  Just overall I would say the final bosses and overall ending were... disappointing.  I don't want to give spoilers, but Gaius and the final DLC boss are just awful.  Endless combos, bad hitboxes, and flashiness to the point of distraction.  It was just so unfun that I had no problem looking up "cheese" methods or even doing a respec just to get through the fights.

Anyway, overall this is one of my favorite games of all time.  It's easily top 5 or maybe even top 2.  I really hope there's an Elden Ring 2 that takes what this game did and even further improves upon it.  I'll be there for it, either way.

review:
Recenzja Bez Spoilerów:
Elden Ring, największy a zarazem najbardziej ambitny projekt od From Software.
Ogromny otwarty świat, setki bossów do pokonania, wiele różnorodnych lokacji do odkrycia...
Ale czy faktycznie jest to takie piękne jak się opisuje?
No... nie do końca,
Do największych plusów Elden Ringa można zaliczyć rzecz jasna system walki który jest dynamiczny, różnorodny (setki dostępnych broni z różnymi animacjami umiejętnościami), oczywiście wymagający, ale i również najprościej rzecz mówiąc... przyjemny.

Z założenia od początku Elden Ring miał być otwartym światem i jak najbardziej ten model kreacji świata w przypadku gatunku souls-like ma swoje podłoże... ALE
Jak tak sięgam przeszłościom to mimo wszystko najprzyjemniej się eksplorowało lokacje zamknięte typowo znane dla dark soulsów (zamki itp.) a właśnie model otwartego świata zwłaszcza tak ogromnego ma zazwyczaj jedną dużą wadę... 💥powtarzalność💥, która niestety i w tym przypadku wadzi.
W grze występuje kilkadziesiąt dungeonów które konstrukcją się specjalnie nie różnią, wystepują oczywiście wyjątki ale i tak model przejścia ich zaczyna nużyć po jakimś czasie.
Powtarzalność przeciwników, szczerze mówiąc to elden ring z wszystkich souls-lików jakie poprzednio grałem (czyli dark souls 1,3) niestety ten problem występuje najczęściej.
Wielu przeciwników jest po prostu kopiowanych i wklejanych do innych różniących od siebie lokacji mimo iż by się prosiło aż o danie tam czegoś nowego.
Jak powtarzalność dungeonów czy przeciwników da się jakoś jeszcze przełknąć tak niestety powtarzalność nagminna bossów to już inna bajka.
Jeśli ktoś z czytających miałby zamiar wyeksplorować całą mapę to musicie mieć z tyłu głowy fakt że z kilkoma bossami będziecie musieli się bić wielokrotnie nie 2-3 razy tylko coś koło 6-7 (i to zaczyna być z czasem naprawdę frustrujące)

Z takich większych minusów to trzeba jeszcze podkreślić zdecydowanie to... jak bardzo gra jest nie intuicyjna dla normalnego gracza który po prostu chciałby się cieszyć rozgrywką. Co mam na myśli?
Questy od npc porozrzucane po całej mapie które tak naprawdę polegają na tym by porozmawiać z jednym potem z drugim potem wrócić do pierwszego itp.
Ale to, że jeden z questów których no nie masz raczej większych szans przejść bez pomocy wyszukiwarki ma w sobie ending możliwy do odblokowania który procentowo jest najczęściej kończony w skali procentowej na steamie, mówi dużo o tym ilu graczy chce zobaczyć całą zawartość gry.

Główny wątek fabularny czy bossowie w większości trzymają tutaj poziom i nie mam się specjalnie czego czepiać, jeśli się będziecie tylko jego trzymać a raczej nie będziecie... dlaczego? 
No bo niestety (bądź stety) jak to w przypadku otwartych światów wygląda gra wymaga poświęcenia sporej ilości czasu eksploracji bez której po prostu będzie bardzo ciężko progresować (zwłaszcza w dlc)
i jak ja nie miałem z tym większego problemu tak w przypadku przeciętnego gracza wyobrażam sobie, że zerkanie w każdy korytarz w tak ogromnym świecie może już nim być i może być przyczyną przez którą opuści grę przed zobaczeniem napisów końcowych.

Co do fabuły to tutaj nie mam specjalnie dużo do powiedzenia, jak to w przypadku gier od From Software fabułe poznajemy czytając opisy przedmiotów, broni, rozmawiając z npc itp.
Mi się osobiście w to nie chciało bawić, i mogę powiedzieć, że jak nie wiedziałem praktycznie nic na początku gry, tak po zakończeniu te huh? również pozostało.
Moim zdaniem najlepszym sposobem na poznanie fabuły jest obejrzenie materiałów na yt o całym lore które są prawdopodobnie jak w przypadku serii dark souls na wysokim poziomie.

Zanim poruszę jeszcze temat dlc chciałbym jeszcze krótko wspomnieć o optymalizacji gry... gra sobie nie radzi z ray traycingiem, gram na nowoczesnym sprzęcie i nie powinien on mieć większych problemów 
z kilkuletnią grą, często występowały przycinki więc musiałem zmniejszyć jakość odbić na niski (próbowałem też z wyłączonym w całości ale wtedy gra wyglądała po prostu tragicznie (rozpikselowanie)) zajęło mi trochę czasu zanim dostosowałem to wszystko żeby było grywalnie. Ogólnie tutaj minus, chociaż jak już rozwiążesz ten problem to gra działa dosyć płynnie, nie licząc efektów wizualnych... np. podczas odradzania się szkieletów w pewnym momencie za każdym razem liczba klatek znacząco spada by ten efekt wyświetlić nwm kto przy tym siedział ale skopał robotę.

Dobra przechodząc do kwestii dlc
Jeśli ktoś się zastanawia nad zakupem z wersją shadow of the erdtree to muszę was przed jedną rzeczą ostrzec... świat zwyczajnie jest pusty, na początku tego nie widać bo ogólnie północ i zachód jest nawet odpowiednio zapełniony tak już cały południowy i wschodni świat to głównie bieganie wierzchowcem (albo w jednej lokacji gdzie gra wymusza biegania na piechotę) po mapie, tam praktycznie niczego niema!
Dlc stara się grać klimatem który na początku działa, ale no jak można doceniać cały czas oprawe wizualną no jak właściwie mam wrażenie że dlc nie wynagradzało odpowiednio eksploracji jednocześnie ją wymuszając zbierając fragmenty ostrodrzewa (które zmniejszają zadawane obrażenia od przeciwników/bossów i jednocześnie zwiększają obrażenia zadawane przez ciebie 😑) czy też fragment czcigodnego ducha (chyba dobrze napisałem (polega on na tym, że robi to samo co fragment ostrodrzewa tylko dla summonów przez ciebie przezywanych)) To jest najgłupszy element wprowadzony w całej grze i nie jestem w stanie go ominąć bokiem. 

Podsumowując:
Jest to dobra gra (momentami wybitna) przejawiająca się swoimi wszelakimi bolączkami otwartego świata.
Jeśli masz już doświadczenie z souls-likami i interesuje ciebie ten tytuł to ostatecznie mogę go polecić (gra dostarczyła wielu godzin przyjemnej trochę innej rozgrywki, ponieważ to w końcu inna formuła kreacji świata). Właśnie dlatego nowym graczom nie obytym w tym gatunku doradziłbym na ten moment nie sięgać po ten tytuł, nie przez trudność, nie przez niektóre bolączki otwartego świata ale przez to właśnie, że moim zdaniem nie jest to typowy souls-like, wyrobisz sobie opinie na temat gatunku czymś czym w sumie nie do końca nim jest. Osobiście poleciłbym zacząć przygodę od serii dark souls (albo części 1 albo części 3  (2 na waszym miejscu bym nie tykał)). I dopiero po tym ruszył w kierunku elden ringa.

review_clean: Recenzja Bez Spoilerów Elden Ring, największy a zarazem najbardziej ambitny projekt od From Software. Ogromny otwarty świat, setki bossów do pokonania, wiele różnorodnych lokacji do odkrycia... Ale czy faktycznie jest to takie piękne jak się opisuje? No... nie do końca, Do największych plusów Elden Ringa można zaliczyć rzecz jasna system walki który jest dynamiczny, różnorodny (setki dostępnych broni z różnymi animacjami umiejętnościami), oczywiście wymagający, ale i również najprościej rzecz mówiąc... przyjemny. Z założenia od początku Elden Ring miał być otwartym światem i jak najbardziej ten model kreacji świata w przypadku gatunku souls-like ma swoje podłoże... ALE Jak tak sięgam przeszłościom to mimo wszystko najprzyjemniej się eksplorowało lokacje zamknięte typowo znane dla dark soulsów (zamki itp.) a właśnie model otwartego świata zwłaszcza tak ogromnego ma zazwyczaj jedną dużą wadę... powtarzalność , która niestety i w tym przypadku wadzi. W grze występuje kilkadziesiąt dungeonów które konstrukcją się specjalnie nie różnią, wystepują oczywiście wyjątki ale i tak model przejścia ich zaczyna nużyć po jakimś czasie. Powtarzalność przeciwników, szczerze mówiąc to elden ring z wszystkich souls-lików jakie poprzednio grałem (czyli dark souls 1,3) niestety ten problem występuje najczęściej. Wielu przeciwników jest po prostu kopiowanych i wklejanych do innych różniących od siebie lokacji mimo iż by się prosiło aż o danie tam czegoś nowego. Jak powtarzalność dungeonów czy przeciwników da się jakoś jeszcze przełknąć tak niestety powtarzalność nagminna bossów to już inna bajka. Jeśli ktoś z czytających miałby zamiar wyeksplorować całą mapę to musicie mieć z tyłu głowy fakt że z kilkoma bossami będziecie musieli się bić wielokrotnie nie 2-3 razy tylko coś koło 6-7 (i to zaczyna być z czasem naprawdę frustrujące) Z takich większych minusów to trzeba jeszcze podkreślić zdecydowanie to... jak bardzo gra jest nie intuicyjna dla normalnego gracza który po prostu chciałby się cieszyć rozgrywką. Co mam na myśli? Questy od npc porozrzucane po całej mapie które tak naprawdę polegają na tym by porozmawiać z jednym potem z drugim potem wrócić do pierwszego itp. Ale to, że jeden z questów których no nie masz raczej większych szans przejść bez pomocy wyszukiwarki ma w sobie ending możliwy do odblokowania który procentowo jest najczęściej kończony w skali procentowej na steamie, mówi dużo o tym ilu graczy chce zobaczyć całą zawartość gry. Główny wątek fabularny czy bossowie w większości trzymają tutaj poziom i nie mam się specjalnie czego czepiać, jeśli się będziecie tylko jego trzymać a raczej nie będziecie... dlaczego? review_english: Review Without Spoilers Elden Ring, the largest and most ambitious project from From Software. A huge open world, hundreds of bosses to defeat, many different locations to discover... But is it really as beautiful as it is described? Well... not entirely. The biggest advantages of Elden Ring include, of course, the combat system, which is dynamic, diverse (hundreds of available weapons with various skill animations), demanding, of course, but also, to put it simply... enjoyable. From the very beginning, Elden Ring was supposed to be an open world, and this model of world creation in the souls-like genre has its basis... BUT, as I go back to the past, it was still the most enjoyable to explore closed locations typical of dark souls (castles, etc.), and the open world model, especially such a huge one, usually has one big drawback... repetition, which, unfortunately, is also a problem in this case. There are several dozen dungeons in the game, the structure of which does not differ much, of course there are exceptions, but the traversal model becomes boring after some time. The repeatability of opponents, to be honest, is the elden ring of all the Souls games I have previously played (i.e. Dark Souls 1.3), unfortunately this problem occurs most often. Many enemies are simply copied and pasted into different locations, even though you would beg for something new to be added there. While the repetitiveness of dungeons and enemies is still tolerable, the constant repetition of bosses is another story. If someone reading this is going to explore the entire map, you must keep in mind the fact that you will have to fight several bosses repeatedly, not 2-3 times, but about 6-7 times (and it starts to get really frustrating over time). Among the major disadvantages, it is worth emphasizing that... how unintuitive the game is for a normal player who just wants to enjoy the game. What do I mean? NPC quests scattered all over the map, which actually involve talking to one person, then another one, then going back to the first one, etc. But the fact that one of the quests that you don't have much chance of completing without the help of a search engine has an unlockable ending that is usually completed on a percentage scale on Steam, says a lot about how many players want to see the entire content of the game. The main storyline and bosses are mostly on the same level here and I don't really have anything to complain about, if you just stick to it or rather not... why? Well, unfortunately (or fortunately), as is the case with open worlds, the game requires spending a lot of time on exploration, without which it will be very difficult to progress (especially in DLC) and while I didn't have any major problems with it, in the case of an average player, I imagine that looking into every corridor in such a huge world may be a problem and may be the reason for leaving the game before seeing the end credits. As for the plot, I don't have much to say here, as in the case of From Software games, we learn the plot by reading descriptions of items, weapons, talking to NPCs, etc. Personally, I didn't want to play it, and I can say that even though I knew practically nothing at the beginning of the game, I didn't know anything after finishing the game, huh? also left. In my opinion, the best way to learn the plot is to watch YouTube materials about the entire lore, which are probably at a high level like in the case of the Dark Souls series. Before I touch on the topic of DLC, I would like to briefly mention the game's optimization... the game doesn't handle ray tracing, I play on modern hardware and it shouldn't have any major problems with a several-year-old game, there were often stutters so I had to reduce the quality of reflections to low (I also tried with it turned off completely, but then the game looked just terrible (pixelated)). It took me some time to adjust it all to make it playable. Overall, this is a minus, although once you solve this problem, the game runs quite smoothly, apart from the visual effects... e.g., when the skeletons are spawning, at some point, the frame rate drops significantly each time to display this effect, no matter who was at it but screwed up the job. Okay, moving on to the DLC issue. If anyone is considering purchasing the Shadow of the Erdtree version, I must warn you about one thing... the world is simply empty, you can't see it at the beginning because the north and west are even adequately filled, so the entire southern and eastern world is mainly about running on a mount (or in one location where the game forces you to run on foot) around the map, there is practically nothing there! The DLC tries to play with the atmosphere that works at the beginning, but how can you appreciate it visuals all the time, well, actually, I have the impression that the DLC did not adequately reward exploration while forcing it by collecting fragments of the holly tree (which reduce the damage dealt by boss opponents and at the same time increase the damage dealt by you) or a fragment of the venerable spirit (I think I wrote it right (it does the same thing as the holly fragment only for summons you call names)) This is the stupidest element introduced in the entire game and I can't get past it sideways. To sum up, it is a good game (at times outstanding) that manifests itself with all the problems of the open world. If you already have experience with Souls games and you are interested in this title, I can ultimately recommend it (the game provided many hours of enjoyable, slightly different gameplay, because it is a different world creation formula). That's why I would advise new players who are not familiar with this genre not to buy this title, not because of the difficulty, not because of some of the problems of the open world, but because, in my opinion, it is not a typical souls-like game, you will form an opinion about the genre with something that it is not entirely. Personally, I would recommend starting your adventure with the Dark Souls series (either part 1 or part 3 (I wouldn't touch part 2 if I were you)). And only after that he moved towards the Elden Ring. My Final Subjective Rating of Elden Ring (Baseline) 8 10 - Combat System 9.5 10 - World Exploration 8.5 10 - Graphics 8.5 10 - Soundtrack 9.5 10 - Main Plot (locations, bosses) 8.5 10 - Side Plots (NPC quests, etc.) 3 10 - Development Characters 9 10 - Endings 7.5 10 Elden Ring (Shadow of The Erdtree (DLC)) 6 10 - Combat System 9.5 10 - World Exploration 6.5 10 - Graphics 8.5 10 - Soundtrack 9 10 - Main Story (locations, bosses) 8 10 - Side Story (quests) npc etc.) 1.5 10 - Character Development 4 10 - Ending 3.5 10 I encourage you to ask questions or share your opinion about the game in the comments. Regards )

review:
心目中最好的魂系游戏。
比起黑暗之魂3，艾尔登法环里具有许多人性化的设计，例如玛莉卡楔石，遍地篝火点，去除了负重单属性加点等。无论是伊鲁席尔还是宁姆格福，老贼的游戏总是充满着美感，从初见史东薇儿城，再到雷亚卢卡利亚学院，魂系独有的“弱指引” 利用这种高大的建筑来吸引玩家去探索，而恰恰老贼的游戏又是真正的“所见即所得”玩家看到的每一个地方 基本都是可以到达的。
艾尔登法环的开放世界魅力在于丰富的内容，也藏着许多秘密。探索未知的洞窟，地牢都能找到不错的收获。难度方面，可以说是历代游戏里“最简单的”魂游，游戏并不强制玩家打完boss才能前进。不像黑暗之魂3那样的线性关卡设计，可以选择先刷级再来挑战boss，更别说本作还自带骨灰系统，可以帮助打boss。
最后，神作无需多言。

review_english: The best Souls game in my mind. Compared with Dark Souls 3, Elden's Circle has many humanized designs, such as the Malika Keystone, bonfire points everywhere, and the removal of single attribute bonus points for load-bearing. Whether it is Irushil or Nimgfu, the old thief's games are always full of beauty. From the first sight of Stonewell City to Rhea Lucalia Academy, the unique weak guidance of the Soul system uses this tall building to attract players to explore. And Cha Cha Lao Thief's game is truly what you see is what you get. Every place the player sees is basically accessible. The charm of the open world of Elden's Ring lies in its rich content and many secrets. You can find good rewards by exploring unknown caves and dungeons. In terms of difficulty, it can be said to be the easiest Soul Game in the past. The game does not force players to defeat the boss before they can move forward. Unlike the linear level design like Dark Souls 3, you can choose to level up first and then challenge the boss. Not to mention that this game also comes with ashes system that can help defeat bosses. Finally, it’s a masterpiece. Needless to say.

The screen fades to black: YOU DIED.

But this is not the end; it is the ultimate teacher. In the grand game of life, failure is inevitable, but it carries a fierce mandate: learn, adapt, and evolve.

Your mistakes are not defeats—they are blueprints for victory.

If you rise from the ashes of your errors and decode their lessons, no wall will be high enough, and no boss fearsome enough to stop you. Study the fall, forge your resolve, and conquer every single obstacle in your path!

I am absolutely obsessed with this game and love the amazing and expansive world you are immersed in from the start, even through my third playthrough I am still managing to find new areas and bosses to fight.

Speaking of bosses, just like in any other souls like game those are the best part and Elden Ring is no exception, all of them are challenging but also super fun to fight (except for the Fire Giant :P) Just PLEASE FromeSoftware add the ability to rematch bosses without having to start a NG+

Over all a super fun game and if you like a fun challenge and willing to spend some money (Worth it BTW) this is a game I would 100% recommend.

review:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Uma das melhores experiências que já tive nos videogames

Elden Ring é uma verdadeira obra-prima e um marco no gênero Soulslike. A FromSoftware conseguiu pegar tudo o que tornou seus jogos anteriores tão especiais e elevar a experiência a um novo nível com um mundo aberto gigantesco, repleto de segredos, desafios e recompensas.

A exploração é simplesmente fantástica. A cada direção que você segue existe algo interessante para encontrar: masmorras ocultas, chefes opcionais, equipamentos poderosos ou paisagens impressionantes. O jogo recompensa constantemente a curiosidade do jogador, tornando cada descoberta memorável.

O combate é extremamente satisfatório e oferece uma enorme variedade de builds. Seja utilizando espadas colossais, magias devastadoras, encantamentos ou armas exóticas, sempre existe uma forma diferente de encarar os desafios. A liberdade para criar seu personagem do jeito que quiser é um dos grandes pontos fortes do jogo.

Visualmente, Elden Ring é impressionante. A direção de arte é espetacular e consegue criar cenários que ficam gravados na memória. Cada região possui sua própria identidade, tornando a jornada pelas Terras Intermédias uma experiência única.

Os chefes são outro destaque. Muitos deles proporcionam batalhas épicas que exigem aprendizado, paciência e habilidade. A sensação de superar um desafio que parecia impossível é algo que poucos jogos conseguem entregar com tanta qualidade.

Além disso, a trilha sonora, a ambientação e o mistério da narrativa criam uma atmosfera envolvente do começo ao fim. É um jogo que respeita a inteligência do jogador e recompensa aqueles que se dedicam a explorar tudo o que ele tem a oferecer.

Elden Ring não é apenas um dos melhores jogos da FromSoftware, mas também um dos melhores RPGs de ação já criados. Altamente recomendado para quem busca aventura, desafio e um mundo fascinante para explorar. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐🗡️🐉👑

review_english: One of the best experiences I've ever had in video games Elden Ring is a true masterpiece and a milestone in the Soulslike genre. FromSoftware has managed to take everything that made its previous games so special and elevate the experience to a new level with a massive open world filled with secrets, challenges, and rewards. The exploration is simply fantastic. In every direction you go there is something interesting to find hidden dungeons, optional bosses, powerful equipment or impressive landscapes. The game constantly rewards the player's curiosity, making each discovery memorable. The combat is extremely satisfying and offers a huge variety of builds. Whether using colossal swords, devastating spells, enchantments or exotic weapons, there is always a different way to face challenges. The freedom to create your character the way you want is one of the game's greatest strengths. Visually, Elden Ring is stunning. The art direction is spectacular and manages to create scenarios that remain engraved in the memory. Each region has its own identity, making the journey through the Middle Lands a unique experience. The bosses are another highlight. Many of them provide epic battles that require learning, patience and skill. The feeling of overcoming a challenge that seemed impossible is something that few games can deliver with such quality. Furthermore, the soundtrack, the setting and the mystery of the narrative create an immersive atmosphere from beginning to end. It's a game that respects the player's intelligence and rewards those who dedicate themselves to exploring everything it has to offer. Elden Ring is not only one of FromSoftware's best games, but also one of the best action RPGs ever created. Highly recommended for anyone looking for adventure, challenge and a fascinating world to explore.

review:
[h1]Melhor [i]Soulslike[/i] que já joguei, sem dúvidas[/h1]

Não há melhor forma de começar no mundo dos [i]Soulslikes[/i] do que jogando Elden Ring. O jogo é uma obra prima em tudo que se propõe a fazer, os defeitos são poucos e não chegam a causar incômodo. 
A gameplay é o ponto mais forte do jogo, com um combate completo e muito satisfatório. A curva de aprendizagem dele é magnífica, no começo vc estranha e até fica um pouco frustrado, mas depois vc vai aprendendo e fica viciado. 
A quantidade de armas e skills fazem surgir infinitas possibilidades de como jogar, e testar novas builds é sempre divertido.
Muitas pessoas reclamam dos gráficos, mas eles fazem parte da atmosfera que aquele universo quer te passar, sem contar que a maioria das reclamações é frescura. A trilha sonora e mundo aberto são brilhantes, ao ponto de você parar e ficar alguns minutos admirando um cenário ou ouvindo uma música.
Além de tudo isso, ainda tem a DLC Shadow of the Erdtree que dizem ser até melhor que o jogo base, então conteúdo é o que não falta. A FromSoftware é uma empresa sem comparação!

[b]Joguei e platinei Elden Ring com um amigo e a experiência foi fantástica. Acho que, se tivesse jogado sozinho, não teria sido tão boa; jogar com amigos deixa qualquer jogo melhor![/b]

[h1][b]GOTY[/b][/h1]

review:
Depois de zerar Dark Souls III veio Elden Ring, e a sensação de liberdade que esse jogo proporciona é algo realmente incrível. Pela primeira vez em um jogo da FromSoftware, você pode simplesmente escolher uma direção e seguir seu caminho, explorando o mundo no seu próprio ritmo.

Não importa para onde você vá, sempre existe algo interessante para descobrir: uma caverna escondida, um chefe opcional, uma nova arma ou uma paisagem de tirar o fôlego. Os cenários são lindos e fazem a exploração ser tão divertida quanto o próprio combate.

O melhor de tudo é poder jogar com calma, sem pressa, aproveitando cada canto do mapa e cada descoberta. Elden Ring recompensa a curiosidade do jogador como poucos jogos conseguem fazer.

Para mim, é uma evolução perfeita da fórmula Souls, juntando a dificuldade e o sentimento de conquista dos jogos anteriores com um mundo aberto espetacular.

Uma verdadeira obra-prima da FromSoftware. ⚔️🌳🐎🎮 10/10.

review_english: After finishing Dark Souls III came Elden Ring, and the feeling of freedom that this game provides is truly incredible. For the first time in a FromSoftware game, you can simply choose a direction and follow your path, exploring the world at your own pace. No matter where you go, there's always something interesting to discover—a hidden cave, an optional boss, a new weapon, or a breathtaking landscape. The settings are beautiful and make exploration as fun as the combat itself. The best of all is being able to play calmly, without rushing, enjoying every corner of the map and every discovery. Elden Ring rewards the player's curiosity like few games can. For me, it's a perfect evolution of the Souls formula, combining the difficulty and feeling of achievement of previous games with a spectacular open world. A true masterpiece from FromSoftware. 10 10.

review:
132h不算长，但终于尘埃落定，该来细数你的罪恶了。能持续玩下去这个游戏的无非三种人，一种是缺爱，需要靠一个看上去很能彰显自我价值的时尚单品来证明自己。一种是抖M，明知道自己正在受戏弄却反而越来越爽的。再有一种就是真的脾气好到了可以包容这种猎奇产品一切缺点的程度了，对任何恶意和缺陷都能最大限度宽容的圣人。但可惜我不属于以上任何一种，我既没有包装自己的需求、也没有受虐上瘾的癖好，并且也没有那个气量去惯着你作为一个商品这样那样的臭毛病。
  从一开始我就抱着尝试的心态，并且告诉自己“不要为难自己，先玩下去再谈是否好玩”，如此看来至少在前期，我的期待没有被辜负——广阔的地图、壮丽的风景、难度适中的战斗、很有阵仗和排场的boss以及得胜之后的征服感，看得出来这些东西有多吸引人。这些东西就是不可被替代的高水平，哪怕从来没有接触过同系列甚至任何玩法相似的其他作品也能直接感受到。
  但可惜在游戏性上还存在着太多足以毁掉一锅汤的老鼠屎。有关魂游这个很模糊的概念相关的那些认知和有争议的部分放到后面细说，先来说说最基本的，让人难以接受的设计失误。碎星拉塔恩，我主观上对这个角色只能说无感，甚至在开始玩这游戏之前刷到boss过场动画的时候还觉得挺霸气的，但是自从我一周目体验过了原汁原味的碎星boss战…毫不夸张地说，那是我第一次在这个游戏里有种吃到屎的感觉。很多时候负反馈很大程度上不是来源于死亡，而是那种被戏弄的羞辱感。单论死亡次数，个人体感下在不熟悉的时候，满月女王的翻车点都比碎星要多，我在满月卡的时间也几乎是前期几个boss里最久的了。但为什么拉塔恩能让我打过之后就碰都不想再碰呢？我认为是敷衍。我抛开可能是魂游特色一部分的开局架大狙射我、像是没上过学的小孩想出来的箭雨招式、批发不要钱的抽水马桶AOE以及看起来很不符合逻辑的大体形高机动性（你的意思是这个长得跟猩猩一样的玩意儿骑着一匹脖子还没他手臂粗的瘦马跑起来很灵活的同时还能快速转圈和追击？）整个boss场地又大又空我不知道是有何用意，为了boss演出看起来比较有排场？就这场boss战的水准我只能说感受不到任何演出方面给的情绪价值。整场战斗就是在这一片红不拉几的大沙滩上瞎跑，拼命找外援的符，偌大的沙滩需要来回跑动复活外援，还要时刻提防某个超雄大猩猩骑着那匹跑车一样的马窜过来发神经砸我两下。如果只是比较依赖临场判断、随机性比较强、机制容错比较低，我都可以很勉强地说服一下自己是自己没处理好。诶，但是您猜怎么着？我新档原来准备做菈妮支线绕不开碎星，然后这一次就让我发现了一坨大的。首先，这场战斗里的外援npc即使战死也可以通过符来重新召唤，所以一般情况下极致稳妥的打法可能是通过符来反复把npc拉起来，把boss耗死。到这里为止都很正常对吧？直到我看见一个刚复活的npc保持着跑动的动作卡在了场地的障碍上。对，就是那个开局可以用来挡碎星大狙的障碍物，场地上有很多。我还得亲自动手去把障碍打掉，这帮弱智外援才走得动路。不好意思，这时候我实在没法说服自己这是一道考验了，什么叫你想营造一个声势浩大的车轮战场面结果参与大战的人员连他妈遇到路障拐个弯都不会？再配上这个boss的各种野蛮得要死的大范围攻击手段，那可太享受了。宫崎英高小时候参加校运会接力赛，接棒的队友踢到一个跟自己脚趾头一般大的小石子就直接原地罚站了，必须要小高自己去把小石子踢开队友才跑得动。
  接着来说地图关卡这一块，按理说应该是吹这款游戏的那帮人最有底气的部分了吧?广受好评的箱庭、四通八达的巧妙路线设计，为了恶作剧而刻意设计的初见杀/转角处决，在道具边上塞致死量的小怪诱惑我用命去换，误人子弟“一周目尽量不看攻略跑图”以保障沉浸感的（哥们都要不知道接下来能往哪走了你还在强调引导破碎是游戏的一部分吗？不知道从哪冒出来的遗老就是见不得新人玩得比自己舒服，这就是典型的第一类人了，缺爱，自己淋过雨就要求后来者也不准打伞，神经病来的。）那些“玩家”，你们都是一丘之貉。我说地图恶心，他们要说“有轮椅呀”，我说所谓的轮椅（实际上这个游戏根本不存在任何一个能够称作轮椅的物品，有些东西我能拿得到的时候就已经不怎么需要它了，而那些相对易于获取的，严谨地来说只能称作强力武器/物品，远没有达到能让人抛弃大脑的程度）都有获取门槛，他们说“探索一下就找到了（就好像上学时父母和你说“努力一下就轻松了”一样扯淡），这时候就已经没有沟通的必要了，有些人已经被魂游这种由童年不幸的孤儿设计出的残缺产品驯化了，也许早就对恶意和愚蠢失去了判断力。在这一作我们的boss强度会相对的高，因为我们提供了骨灰这种选项；那为了防止玩家体会不到老魂游的劲道怎么办呢？那当然是给骨灰一个独立的等级系统了，至于前期怎么升级骨灰？那当然是探索我们经典的地下墓地来采花，感受最纯正的恶作剧了，给你塞点屎进来好让你别忘了艾尔登法环是一款魂系游戏。我就问一个问题：你配吗？
  针对这种阴暗闭塞的小图，社区的态度似乎更多是调侃而非攻击，诸如地下墓地、各种洞窟、雾霾大雪原、王城臭水沟这种几乎是屎尿屁级别的设计，大多数人在讨论时形成了一种怪风气，就是会去比哪一个更粪，以至于忘了攻击一下作为罪魁祸首的设计者——在我看来属实是给你脸给多了。
